{"id":"5c39b383-018d-4b3b-97c7-d9a5129c119d","icon":null,"color":"#bec2c8","name":"Hey Chill","summary":"Voice-first AI companion — dogfooding OpenClaw","description":"## Final Goal

Kim uses it daily for hands-free interaction with their agents.

## Approach (revised 2026-03-05)

Standalone PWA — decoupled from AIRI. Installable on phone/tablet/desktop.

* Always-on wake word (Hey Chill) OR push-to-talk button
* Voice → transcription → send to Chill via Slack (n8n webhook)
* No Live2D, no AIRI complexity — just a mic and a button
* AIRI repo preserved for later if needed

## Live URL

[https://brainwave-ten.vercel.app](<https://brainwave-ten.vercel.app>) (deployed as Brainwave)

## Repo

chillkimtest-oss/hey-chill

---

## Features (cumulative — updated after each milestone)

### Push-to-Talk

* Press and hold button to record voice
* Uses Web Speech API for real-time transcription
* Visual feedback during recording

### Voice Activity Detection (VAD)

* Auto-stops recording after 3.5 seconds of silence
* Allows natural pauses between sentences without cutting off

### Transcription

* Real-time speech-to-text via Web Speech API
* Interim results shown during recording
* Final transcript captured on recording end

### Slack Integration

* Sends transcription to Chill agent via n8n webhook
* Webhook URL: /webhook/hey-chill
* Message delivered to Slack channel

### PWA

* Installable as progressive web app
* Service worker for offline shell
* Works on mobile and desktop browsers","url":"https://linear.app/chills-linear/project/hey-chill-335516e3da6b","createdAt":"2026-03-01T02:51:01.776Z","updatedAt":"2026-03-07T23:21:25.560Z","startDate":"2026-03-01","targetDate":null,"labels":[],"initiatives":[],"lead":{},"status":{"id":"45fae361-8f51-4684-a479-68682e997bd2","name":"In Progress"},"teams":[{"id":"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd","name":"Forge","key":"FRG"}]}

{"issues":[{"id":"207a955d-7044-4ef7-bb0a-b6b712180f56","identifier":"FRG-237","title":"Set up QA regression suite for Hey Chill","description":"## Context

Hey Chill M1 is complete. Before M2 starts, we need automated regression tests.

## What to do

1. Read the project description in Linear — it contains the cumulative feature spec.
2. Set up testing in the main repo (chillkimtest-oss/hey-chill). Playwright recommended for PWA testing.
3. Write regression tests covering: push-to-talk UI, VAD silence timeout (3.5s), transcription display, Slack webhook send, PWA installability, service… (truncated, use `get_issue` for full description)","priority":{"value":2,"name":"High"},"url":"https://linear.app/chills-linear/issue/FRG-237/set-up-qa-regression-suite-for-hey-chill","gitBranchName":"chillkimtest/frg-237-set-up-qa-regression-suite-for-hey-chill","createdAt":"2026-03-07T23:22:09.633Z","updatedAt":"2026-03-08T02:29:03.513Z","archivedAt":null,"completedAt":"2026-03-08T02:29:03.494Z","dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"Done","labels":["testing"],"createdBy":"Chill","createdById":"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7","project":"Hey Chill","projectId":"5c39b383-018d-4b3b-97c7-d9a5129c119d","team":"Forge","teamId":"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd"},{"id":"2d07ba50-fca1-40b1-82f5-d758751126da","identifier":"FRG-210","title":"Bug: Idle time to auto-stop recording is too short","description":"The VAD (voice activity detection) silence timeout stops recording too quickly. Kim reports it cuts off before finishing a thought. Increase the idle timeout threshold so users have more time to pause between sentences without the recording stopping.","projectMilestone":{"id":"fa68e282-c449-402b-b1dc-c64e4a35d70e","name":"M1: Voice-to-Slack works"},"priority":{"value":1,"name":"Urgent"},"url":"https://linear.app/chills-linear/issue/FRG-210/bug-idle-time-to-auto-stop-recording-is-too-short","gitBranchName":"chillkimtest/frg-210-bug-idle-time-to-auto-stop-recording-is-too-short","createdAt":"2026-03-07T01:15:43.345Z","updatedAt":"2026-03-07T22:39:37.674Z","archivedAt":null,"completedAt":"2026-03-07T22:39:37.659Z","dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"Done","labels":[],"createdBy":"Chill","createdById":"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7","assignee":"Chill","assigneeId":"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7","project":"Hey Chill","projectId":"5c39b383-018d-4b3b-97c7-d9a5129c119d","team":"Forge","teamId":"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd"},{"id":"ea9a6b42-b9e5-4ec3-bb58-c1f15e584b2f","identifier":"FRG-214","title":"Brainstorm post-processing: readable summary from raw transcript","description":"After brainstorm recording stops, process the raw transcript into a clean readable format. Reference Brainwave repo for the summarization approach. Show both raw and processed versions. Send the processed version to Slack.","projectMilestone":{"id":"8151d407-d1e0-4674-8fbe-d243e5be4895","name":"M3: Daily driver — dual mode"},"priority":{"value":2,"name":"High"},"url":"https://linear.app/chills-linear/issue/FRG-214/brainstorm-post-processing-readable-summary-from-raw-transcript","gitBranchName":"chillkimtest/frg-214-brainstorm-post-processing-readable-summary-from-raw","createdAt":"2026-03-07T01:15:44.498Z","updatedAt":"2026-03-07T01:15:44.498Z","archivedAt":null,"completedAt":null,"dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"Backlog","labels":[],"createdBy":"Chill","createdById":"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7","project":"Hey Chill","projectId":"5c39b383-018d-4b3b-97c7-d9a5129c119d","team":"Forge","teamId":"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd"},{"id":"bb9b7691-c6f9-4753-8e88-bf8cc3c2aaf6","identifier":"FRG-213","title":"Language support: Cantonese and English","description":"Transcription must handle both Cantonese and English speech. Auto-detect or allow user to select language. Web Speech API supports zh-HK for Cantonese. Test with mixed-language input.","projectMilestone":{"id":"8151d407-d1e0-4674-8fbe-d243e5be4895","name":"M3: Daily driver — dual mode"},"priority":{"value":2,"name":"High"},"url":"https://linear.app/chills-linear/issue/FRG-213/language-support-cantonese-and-english","gitBranchName":"chillkimtest/frg-213-language-support-cantonese-and-english","createdAt":"2026-03-07T01:15:44.254Z","updatedAt":"2026-03-07T01:15:44.254Z","archivedAt":null,"completedAt":null,"dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"Backlog","labels":[],"createdBy":"Chill","createdById":"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7","project":"Hey Chill","projectId":"5c39b383-018d-4b3b-97c7-d9a5129c119d","team":"Forge","teamId":"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd"},{"id":"4e278ef1-9dac-4b12-9f9a-07e98595f024","identifier":"FRG-212","title":"Brainstorm mode: continuous recording with real-time transcript","description":"No VAD in brainstorm mode — record continuously until Stop is pressed. Show real-time transcript while speaking (reference Brainwave repo for implementation). On stop, produce both raw transcript and a cleaned readable version.","projectMilestone":{"id":"8151d407-d1e0-4674-8fbe-d243e5be4895","name":"M3: Daily driver — dual mode"},"priority":{"value":2,"name":"High"},"url":"https://linear.app/chills-linear/issue/FRG-212/brainstorm-mode-continuous-recording-with-real-time-transcript","gitBranchName":"chillkimtest/frg-212-brainstorm-mode-continuous-recording-with-real-time","createdAt":"2026-03-07T01:15:44.002Z","updatedAt":"2026-03-07T01:15:44.002Z","archivedAt":null,"completedAt":null,"dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"Backlog","labels":[],"createdBy":"Chill","createdById":"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7","project":"Hey Chill","projectId":"5c39b383-018d-4b3b-97c7-d9a5129c119d","team":"Forge","teamId":"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd"},{"id":"b3acd63c-9d87-4000-8190-74646fe3767e","identifier":"FRG-211","title":"Dual-mode UI: Quick Request (A) + Brainstorm (B) buttons","description":"Replace single mic button with two distinct buttons. Button A = Quick Request (current push-to-talk with VAD). Button B = Brainstorm (continuous recording, manual stop). Clear visual distinction between modes.","projectMilestone":{"id":"8151d407-d1e0-4674-8fbe-d243e5be4895","name":"M3: Daily driver — dual mode"},"priority":{"value":2,"name":"High"},"url":"https://linear.app/chills-linear/issue/FRG-211/dual-mode-ui-quick-request-a-brainstorm-b-buttons","gitBranchName":"chillkimtest/frg-211-dual-mode-ui-quick-request-a-brainstorm-b-buttons","createdAt":"2026-03-07T01:15:43.753Z","updatedAt":"2026-03-07T01:15:43.753Z","archivedAt":null,"completedAt":null,"dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"Backlog","labels":[],"createdBy":"Chill","createdById":"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7","project":"Hey Chill","projectId":"5c39b383-018d-4b3b-97c7-d9a5129c119d","team":"Forge","teamId":"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd"},{"id":"48f4635e-d5ac-4022-ae0e-640637ce60e8","identifier":"FRG-188","title":"Send transcription to Chill via Slack webhook","description":"After transcription, send the text to Chill agent via Slack.

Route: PWA → n8n webhook (or direct Slack incoming webhook) → Chill channel in Slack.

Acceptance: speak into PWA, message appears in Slack, Chill responds.","projectMilestone":{"id":"fa68e282-c449-402b-b1dc-c64e4a35d70e","name":"M1: Voice-to-Slack works"},"priority":{"value":1,"name":"Urgent"},"url":"https://linear.app/chills-linear/issue/FRG-188/send-transcription-to-chill-via-slack-webhook","gitBranchName":"chillkimtest/frg-188-send-transcription-to-chill-via-slack-webhook","createdAt":"2026-03-06T04:29:08.136Z","updatedAt":"2026-03-06T17:53:57.893Z","archivedAt":null,"completedAt":"2026-03-06T17:53:57.877Z","dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"Done","labels":[],"createdBy":"Chill","createdById":"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7","project":"Hey Chill","projectId":"5c39b383-018d-4b3b-97c7-d9a5129c119d","team":"Forge","teamId":"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd"},{"id":"a5e8602b-2e89-4bbe-ae3d-9641a0076649","identifier":"FRG-189","title":"Deploy Brainwave for Kim to evaluate","description":"Deploy grapeot/brainwave (Python FastAPI + WebSocket) so Kim can test the speech-to-organized-notes workflow.

Cannot use Vercel (WebSocket requirement). Deploy via Docker on home server behind Cloudflare tunnel.

Acceptance: Kim can access Brainwave at a URL and test voice brainstorming.","projectMilestone":{"id":"fa68e282-c449-402b-b1dc-c64e4a35d70e","name":"M1: Voice-to-Slack works"},"priority":{"value":2,"name":"High"},"url":"https://linear.app/chills-linear/issue/FRG-189/deploy-brainwave-for-kim-to-evaluate","gitBranchName":"chillkimtest/frg-189-deploy-brainwave-for-kim-to-evaluate","createdAt":"2026-03-06T04:29:08.401Z","updatedAt":"2026-03-06T04:41:28.941Z","archivedAt":null,"completedAt":"2026-03-06T04:41:28.924Z","dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"Done","labels":[],"createdBy":"Chill","createdById":"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7","project":"Hey Chill","projectId":"5c39b383-018d-4b3b-97c7-d9a5129c119d","team":"Forge","teamId":"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd"},{"id":"95c1bfc6-dcb0-4cf5-8805-2d818fa61b7b","identifier":"FRG-187","title":"Push-to-talk: capture voice and transcribe","description":"Tap the mic button → record audio → transcribe via Web Speech API or OpenAI Whisper.

Display transcription on screen. User can review before sending.

Acceptance: tap button, speak, see accurate transcription in the UI.","projectMilestone":{"id":"fa68e282-c449-402b-b1dc-c64e4a35d70e","name":"M1: Voice-to-Slack works"},"priority":{"value":1,"name":"Urgent"},"url":"https://linear.app/chills-linear/issue/FRG-187/push-to-talk-capture-voice-and-transcribe","gitBranchName":"chillkimtest/frg-187-push-to-talk-capture-voice-and-transcribe","createdAt":"2026-03-06T04:29:07.902Z","updatedAt":"2026-03-06T04:41:28.714Z","archivedAt":null,"completedAt":"2026-03-06T04:41:28.692Z","dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"Done","labels":[],"createdBy":"Chill","createdById":"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7","project":"Hey Chill","projectId":"5c39b383-018d-4b3b-97c7-d9a5129c119d","team":"Forge","teamId":"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd"},{"id":"38b5400f-7856-44de-8e39-4ab219d148e2","identifier":"FRG-186","title":"Scaffold hey-chill PWA repo","description":"Create chillkimtest-oss/hey-chill repo.

Minimal PWA scaffold:

* index.html with mic button
* manifest.json for installability
* Service worker for offline
* Deploy to Vercel

Acceptance: installable PWA loads on mobile/desktop with a visible mic button.","projectMilestone":{"id":"fa68e282-c449-402b-b1dc-c64e4a35d70e","name":"M1: Voice-to-Slack works"},"priority":{"value":1,"name":"Urgent"},"url":"https://linear.app/chills-linear/issue/FRG-186/scaffold-hey-chill-pwa-repo","gitBranchName":"chillkimtest/frg-186-scaffold-hey-chill-pwa-repo","createdAt":"2026-03-06T04:29:07.653Z","updatedAt":"2026-03-06T04:41:28.202Z","archivedAt":null,"completedAt":"2026-03-06T04:41:28.099Z","dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"Done","labels":[],"createdBy":"Chill","createdById":"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7","project":"Hey Chill","projectId":"5c39b383-018d-4b3b-97c7-d9a5129c119d","team":"Forge","teamId":"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd"},{"id":"c7af639a-39e7-48ef-885a-2edfaf79f921","identifier":"FRG-173","title":"Wire voice commands to OpenClaw gateway for agent routing","description":"## Context

After wake word detection, transcribed voice commands need to reach OpenClaw which routes to Chill (daily ops, calendar, ideas) or other agents.

## Requirements

* Kuma sends transcribed text to OpenClaw chat completions endpoint
* OpenClaw handles routing to the right agent
* Response text fed to ElevenLabs TTS for spoken reply
* Depends on: wake word detection ticket + FRG-159 (gateway networking)","projectMilestone":{"id":"fa68e282-c449-402b-b1dc-c64e4a35d70e","name":"M1: Voice-to-Slack works"},"priority":{"value":2,"name":"High"},"url":"https://linear.app/chills-linear/issue/FRG-173/wire-voice-commands-to-openclaw-gateway-for-agent-routing","gitBranchName":"chillkimtest/frg-173-wire-voice-commands-to-openclaw-gateway-for-agent-routing","createdAt":"2026-03-03T06:34:07.533Z","updatedAt":"2026-03-06T04:28:46.547Z","archivedAt":null,"completedAt":null,"dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"Canceled","labels":[],"createdBy":"Chill","createdById":"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7","project":"Hey Chill","projectId":"5c39b383-018d-4b3b-97c7-d9a5129c119d","team":"Forge","teamId":"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd"},{"id":"dfc014ba-53f1-469d-adf6-2cf72cec2c12","identifier":"FRG-172","title":"Implement wake word detection (\"Hey Kuma\") via Web Speech API continuous mode","description":"## Context

Kuma (AIRI fork) already has VAD, STT, continuous listening, and ElevenLabs TTS. Missing piece: always-on wake word detection.

## Approach (Phase 1)

Use Web Speech API continuous mode (already in AIRI) + string-match for 'Hey Kuma' prefix. Strip prefix, send rest to OpenClaw as voice command. Zero new deps.

## Phase 2 (future)

Upgrade to Picovoice Porcupine for lower battery drain and better accuracy (free tier: 1 user, 1 custom … (truncated, use `get_issue` for full description)","projectMilestone":{"id":"fa68e282-c449-402b-b1dc-c64e4a35d70e","name":"M1: Voice-to-Slack works"},"priority":{"value":2,"name":"High"},"url":"https://linear.app/chills-linear/issue/FRG-172/implement-wake-word-detection-hey-kuma-via-web-speech-api-continuous","gitBranchName":"chillkimtest/frg-172-implement-wake-word-detection-hey-kuma-via-web-speech-api","createdAt":"2026-03-03T06:34:07.304Z","updatedAt":"2026-03-06T04:28:46.351Z","archivedAt":null,"completedAt":null,"dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"Canceled","labels":[],"createdBy":"Chill","createdById":"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7","project":"Hey Chill","projectId":"5c39b383-018d-4b3b-97c7-d9a5129c119d","team":"Forge","teamId":"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd","cycleId":"5c019f03-3dc2-4ef1-9b52-2ab2b6f9dba4"},{"id":"c336fd33-3782-4c85-adf0-3b20bd3b0800","identifier":"FRG-159","title":"Expose OpenClaw gateway for AIRI web app (networking/CORS)","description":"## Problem

The AIRI web app ([https://airi-deploy.vercel.app](<https://airi-deploy.vercel.app>)) runs on HTTPS but the OpenClaw gateway runs on HTTP localhost. Browsers block mixed content (HTTPS→HTTP). Also the gateway lacks CORS headers and a /v1/models endpoint.

## What Worked (hack)

A Node.js CORS proxy on port 18790 that:

* Adds CORS headers to all responses
* Serves a fake /v1/models response
* Proxies /v1/chat/completions to the gatew… (truncated, use `get_issue` for full description)","priority":{"value":2,"name":"High"},"url":"https://linear.app/chills-linear/issue/FRG-159/expose-openclaw-gateway-for-airi-web-app-networkingcors","gitBranchName":"chillkimtest/frg-159-expose-openclaw-gateway-for-airi-web-app-networkingcors","createdAt":"2026-03-01T05:13:04.918Z","updatedAt":"2026-03-04T03:49:13.157Z","archivedAt":null,"completedAt":"2026-03-03T05:55:39.351Z","dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"Done","labels":[],"createdBy":"Chill","createdById":"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7","assignee":"kimwong.wwk@gmail.com","assigneeId":"fd40b97e-c958-48cb-8745-e5e9f9ac768f","project":"Hey Chill","projectId":"5c39b383-018d-4b3b-97c7-d9a5129c119d","team":"Forge","teamId":"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd"},{"id":"f24f9f80-e121-4f0f-a97a-cd091e514b76","identifier":"FRG-157","title":"Configure Chill character appearance in AIRI","description":"Appearance only — personality handled by OpenClaw (FRG-160 done).

Options for Chill avatar:

1. Use bundled AvatarSample_A/B.vrm (quick, generic)
2. Download free VRM from VRoid Hub ([hub.vroid.com](<http://hub.vroid.com>)) or [Booth.pm](<http://Booth.pm>) (\\~1884 free VRM models on Booth)
3. Create custom in VRoid Studio (free, best result, needs GUI)

AIRI character config: Settings > Characters > Create. Supports VRM and Live2D.

Key files: … (truncated, use `get_issue` for full description)","priority":{"value":3,"name":"Medium"},"url":"https://linear.app/chills-linear/issue/FRG-157/configure-chill-character-appearance-in-airi","gitBranchName":"chillkimtest/frg-157-configure-chill-character-appearance-in-airi","createdAt":"2026-03-01T03:30:58.758Z","updatedAt":"2026-03-03T06:24:50.030Z","archivedAt":null,"completedAt":null,"dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"Canceled","labels":[],"createdBy":"Chill","createdById":"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7","project":"Hey Chill","projectId":"5c39b383-018d-4b3b-97c7-d9a5129c119d","team":"Forge","teamId":"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd"},{"id":"b3ce30c6-bdee-4f4c-9469-5d7ee33a8a68","identifier":"FRG-156","title":"Build and package Electron desktop app (stage-tamagotchi)","description":"Cannot build in DevLab (4GB Docker OOM) or on Forge's machine. AIRI monorepo pnpm install alone exceeds 4GB. Needs a machine with 8GB+ RAM.

Blocked until we have a larger build machine or find a way to build stage-tamagotchi in isolation.","priority":{"value":3,"name":"Medium"},"url":"https://linear.app/chills-linear/issue/FRG-156/build-and-package-electron-desktop-app-stage-tamagotchi","gitBranchName":"chillkimtest/frg-156-build-and-package-electron-desktop-app-stage-tamagotchi","createdAt":"2026-03-01T03:30:58.506Z","updatedAt":"2026-03-03T05:56:17.180Z","archivedAt":null,"completedAt":null,"dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"Canceled","labels":["blocked"],"createdBy":"Chill","createdById":"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7","project":"Hey Chill","projectId":"5c39b383-018d-4b3b-97c7-d9a5129c119d","team":"Forge","teamId":"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd"},{"id":"7dd90345-efc8-4a2c-b66d-0e5b4847f697","identifier":"FRG-163","title":"Fix emotion-to-motion mapping for default Live2D model","description":"LLM emits ACT emotion tags but mapping targets non-existent motion groups. Only neutral→Idle works. Need to remap to actual model groups (Idle, Flick, FlickDown, FlickUp, Tap, Tap@Body, Flick@Body) and add direct motion field support.","priority":{"value":1,"name":"Urgent"},"url":"https://linear.app/chills-linear/issue/FRG-163/fix-emotion-to-motion-mapping-for-default-live2d-model","gitBranchName":"chillkimtest/frg-163-fix-emotion-to-motion-mapping-for-default-live2d-model","createdAt":"2026-03-01T21:04:56.514Z","updatedAt":"2026-03-01T21:09:37.404Z","archivedAt":null,"completedAt":"2026-03-01T21:09:37.385Z","dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"Done","labels":[],"createdBy":"Chill","createdById":"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7","project":"Hey Chill","projectId":"5c39b383-018d-4b3b-97c7-d9a5129c119d","team":"Forge","teamId":"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd"},{"id":"48e8991c-32c5-42b4-8ae7-7ddef1bd2cf0","identifier":"FRG-160","title":"OpenClaw provider: skip AIRI character system prompt","description":"AIRI prepends its own character personality prompt to messages sent to the LLM. When using OpenClaw as the provider, this overrides the agent identity defined in [SOUL.md/IDENTITY.md](<http://SOUL.md/IDENTITY.md>) — so the agent thinks it's Airi instead of Chill.

Fix: In the OpenClaw provider's createProvider or streamFrom path, strip/skip the AIRI character system prompt. Let OpenClaw's gateway handle identity entirely.

Key files:

* packages… (truncated, use `get_issue` for full description)","priority":{"value":1,"name":"Urgent"},"url":"https://linear.app/chills-linear/issue/FRG-160/openclaw-provider-skip-airi-character-system-prompt","gitBranchName":"chillkimtest/frg-160-openclaw-provider-skip-airi-character-system-prompt","createdAt":"2026-03-01T18:03:53.697Z","updatedAt":"2026-03-01T19:19:02.400Z","archivedAt":null,"completedAt":"2026-03-01T19:19:02.384Z","dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"Done","labels":[],"createdBy":"Chill","createdById":"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7","project":"Hey Chill","projectId":"5c39b383-018d-4b3b-97c7-d9a5129c119d","team":"Forge","teamId":"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd"},{"id":"a614623d-5774-46dd-87b2-986f5be847fb","identifier":"FRG-162","title":"Connect AIRI repo to Vercel for auto-deploy on push","description":"Vercel needs a GitHub Login Connection to enable auto-deploy.

Steps:

1. Go to [https://vercel.com/account/login-connections](<https://vercel.com/account/login-connections>)
2. Add GitHub connection (OAuth)
3. Go to [https://vercel.com/chills-projects-19d98ccf/airi-deploy/settings/git](<https://vercel.com/chills-projects-19d98ccf/airi-deploy/settings/git>)
4. Connect repo: chillkimtest-oss/airi
5. Set production branch: main

After this, every … (truncated, use `get_issue` for full description)","priority":{"value":2,"name":"High"},"url":"https://linear.app/chills-linear/issue/FRG-162/connect-airi-repo-to-vercel-for-auto-deploy-on-push","gitBranchName":"chillkimtest/frg-162-connect-airi-repo-to-vercel-for-auto-deploy-on-push","createdAt":"2026-03-01T19:02:11.016Z","updatedAt":"2026-03-01T19:08:46.210Z","archivedAt":null,"completedAt":"2026-03-01T19:08:46.192Z","dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"Done","labels":[],"createdBy":"Chill","createdById":"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7","assignee":"kimwong.wwk@gmail.com","assigneeId":"fd40b97e-c958-48cb-8745-e5e9f9ac768f","project":"Hey Chill","projectId":"5c39b383-018d-4b3b-97c7-d9a5129c119d","team":"Forge","teamId":"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd"},{"id":"d6756974-bc18-4705-bc87-969eb371e1f5","identifier":"FRG-161","title":"OpenClaw provider: manual model input instead of /v1/models discovery","description":"The AIRI onboarding tries to fetch /v1/models to populate the model dropdown. OpenClaw gateway doesn't serve this endpoint.

Options:

1. Let user type model name manually (e.g. openclaw:chill) — skip model discovery
2. Add a config field for model name in the provider settings
3. Hardcode a sensible default (openclaw:main)

Option 1 or 2 preferred — don't depend on /v1/models existing.","priority":{"value":1,"name":"Urgent"},"url":"https://linear.app/chills-linear/issue/FRG-161/openclaw-provider-manual-model-input-instead-of-v1models-discovery","gitBranchName":"chillkimtest/frg-161-openclaw-provider-manual-model-input-instead-of-v1models","createdAt":"2026-03-01T18:03:53.953Z","updatedAt":"2026-03-01T18:04:48.444Z","archivedAt":null,"completedAt":"2026-03-01T18:04:48.430Z","dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"Done","labels":[],"createdBy":"Chill","createdById":"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7","project":"Hey Chill","projectId":"5c39b383-018d-4b3b-97c7-d9a5129c119d","team":"Forge","teamId":"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd"},{"id":"e9f902e7-4b93-41af-8240-3b4cc16d09fa","identifier":"FRG-155","title":"Deploy stage-web to Vercel","description":"Deploy the AIRI stage-web PWA from our fork to Vercel. This gives us a live web version to test the OpenClaw integration.","priority":{"value":2,"name":"High"},"url":"https://linear.app/chills-linear/issue/FRG-155/deploy-stage-web-to-vercel","gitBranchName":"chillkimtest/frg-155-deploy-stage-web-to-vercel","createdAt":"2026-03-01T03:30:58.273Z","updatedAt":"2026-03-01T04:23:55.991Z","archivedAt":null,"completedAt":"2026-03-01T04:23:55.972Z","dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"Done","labels":[],"createdBy":"Chill","createdById":"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7","project":"Hey Chill","projectId":"5c39b383-018d-4b3b-97c7-d9a5129c119d","team":"Forge","teamId":"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd"},{"id":"88b6cc35-8149-4c7f-8b9f-370c81460e9b","identifier":"FRG-154","title":"Build OpenClaw adapter for AIRI's LLM layer","description":"Implement OpenClaw gateway WebSocket connection that satisfies AIRI's backend interface. Right-click menu: Show/Hide, Settings, Quit. Double-click toggles visibility. Stays within display bounds. Draggable by user. Saves/restores position via electron-store. Handle multi-monitor.","priority":{"value":2,"name":"High"},"url":"https://linear.app/chills-linear/issue/FRG-145/character-movement-along-screen-edges","gitBranchName":"chillkimtest/frg-145-character-movement-along-screen-edges","createdAt":"2026-03-01T02:54:32.620Z","updatedAt":"2026-03-01T02:54:32.620Z","archivedAt":"2026-03-01T02:58:58.063Z","completedAt":null,"dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"Todo","labels":[],"createdBy":"Chill","createdById":"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7","project":"Hey Chill","projectId":"5c39b383-018d-4b3b-97c7-d9a5129c119d","team":"Forge","teamId":"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd"},{"id":"abaac5fc-802f-4b3f-97a1-60a714ffbaaf","identifier":"FRG-144","title":"Build character sprite renderer + animation system","description":"Canvas or PixiJS sprite renderer in Electron renderer process. Load sprite sheet, play animations, handle state transitions (idle → walk → react → idle). Throttle to 4fps idle, 30fps active.","priority":{"value":2,"name":"High"},"url":"https://linear.app/chills-linear/issue/FRG-144/build-character-sprite-renderer-animation-system","gitBranchName":"chillkimtest/frg-144-build-character-sprite-renderer-animation-system","createdAt":"2026-03-01T02:54:32.374Z","updatedAt":"2026-03-01T02:54:32.374Z","archivedAt":"2026-03-01T02:58:57.640Z","completedAt":null,"dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"Todo","labels":[],"createdBy":"Chill","createdById":"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7","project":"Hey Chill","projectId":"5c39b383-018d-4b3b-97c7-d9a5129c119d","team":"Forge","teamId":"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd"},{"id":"776cd7a2-4819-4a81-be3c-3ce53e62f867","identifier":"FRG-143","title":"Create pixel art Chill sprite sheet","description":"Design 32x32 or 48x48 pixel art character sprites for Chill. Export as sprite sheet PNG.","priority":{"value":2,"name":"High"},"url":"https://linear.app/chills-linear/issue/FRG-143/create-pixel-art-chill-sprite-sheet","gitBranchName":"chillkimtest/frg-143-create-pixel-art-chill-sprite-sheet","createdAt":"2026-03-01T02:54:32.164Z","updatedAt":"2026-03-01T02:54:32.164Z","archivedAt":"2026-03-01T02:58:57.251Z","completedAt":null,"dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"Todo","labels":[],"createdBy":"Chill","createdById":"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7","project":"Hey Chill","projectId":"5c39b383-018d-4b3b-97c7-d9a5129c119d","team":"Forge","teamId":"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd"},{"id":"bedc0d30-723a-4572-8587-cee35d9d0df5","identifier":"FRG-142","title":"Implement transparent click-through overlay window","description":"BrowserWindow with transparent: true, frame: false. Click-through with setIgnoreMouseEvents forwarding. Character sprite area should capture clicks, rest passes through. Reference: AIRI stage-tamagotchi.","priority":{"value":1,"name":"Urgent"},"url":"https://linear.app/chills-linear/issue/FRG-142/implement-transparent-click-through-overlay-window","gitBranchName":"chillkimtest/frg-142-implement-transparent-click-through-overlay-window","createdAt":"2026-03-01T02:54:31.922Z","updatedAt":"2026-03-01T02:54:31.922Z","archivedAt":"2026-03-01T02:58:56.771Z","completedAt":null,"dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"Todo","labels":[],"createdBy":"Chill","createdById":"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7","project":"Hey Chill","projectId":"5c39b383-018d-4b3b-97c7-d9a5129c119d","team":"Forge","teamId":"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd"},{"id":"b2d87708-b8fa-4683-b94f-0973dd89340b","identifier":"FRG-141","title":"Scaffold Electron + Vite + TypeScript project","description":"Set up Electron app with Vite bundler, TypeScript, pnpm. Transparent frameless BrowserWindow. Basic main/renderer process split.   "content": {
    "tool_use_id": "toolu_012ATYspgYFVmyxrZ2mrLv9A",
    "type": "tool_result",
    "content": [
      {
        "type": "text",
        "text": "{\"issues\":[{\"id\":\"207a955d-7044-4ef7-bb0a-b6b712180f56\",\"identifier\":\"FRG-237\",\"title\":\"Set up QA regression suite for Hey Chill\",\"description\":\"## Context\\n\\nHey Chill M1 is complete. Before M2 starts, we need automated regression tests.\\n\\n## What to do\\n\\n1. Read the project description in Linear \u2014 it contains the cumulative feature spec.\\n2. Set up testing in the main repo (chillkimtest-oss/hey-chill). Playwright recommended for PWA testing.\\n3. Write regression tests covering: push-to-talk UI, VAD silence timeout (3.5s), transcription display, Slack webhook send, PWA installability, service\u2026 (truncated, use `get_issue` for full description)\",\"priority\":{\"value\":2,\"name\":\"High\"},\"url\":\"https://linear.app/chills-linear/issue/FRG-237/set-up-qa-regression-suite-for-hey-chill\",\"gitBranchName\":\"chillkimtest/frg-237-set-up-qa-regression-suite-for-hey-chill\",\"createdAt\":\"2026-03-07T23:22:09.633Z\",\"updatedAt\":\"2026-03-08T02:29:03.513Z\",\"archivedAt\":null,\"completedAt\":\"2026-03-08T02:29:03.494Z\",\"dueDate\":null,\"slaStartedAt\":null,\"slaMediumRiskAt\":null,\"slaHighRiskAt\":null,\"slaBreachesAt\":null,\"slaType\":\"all\",\"status\":\"Done\",\"labels\":[\"testing\"],\"createdBy\":\"Chill\",\"createdById\":\"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7\",\"project\":\"Hey Chill\",\"projectId\":\"5c39b383-018d-4b3b-97c7-d9a5129c119d\",\"team\":\"Forge\",\"teamId\":\"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd\"},{\"id\":\"2d07ba50-fca1-40b1-82f5-d758751126da\",\"identifier\":\"FRG-210\",\"title\":\"Bug: Idle time to auto-stop recording is too short\",\"description\":\"The VAD (voice activity detection) silence timeout stops recording too quickly. Kim reports it cuts off before finishing a thought. Increase the idle timeout threshold so users have more time to pause between sentences without the recording stopping.\",\"projectMilestone\":{\"id\":\"fa68e282-c449-402b-b1dc-c64e4a35d70e\",\"name\":\"M1: Voice-to-Slack works\"},\"priority\":{\"value\":1,\"name\":\"Urgent\"},\"url\":\"https://linear.app/chills-linear/issue/FRG-210/bug-idle-time-to-auto-stop-recording-is-too-short\",\"gitBranchName\":\"chillkimtest/frg-210-bug-idle-time-to-auto-stop-recording-is-too-short\",\"createdAt\":\"2026-03-07T01:15:43.345Z\",\"updatedAt\":\"2026-03-07T22:39:37.674Z\",\"archivedAt\":null,\"completedAt\":\"2026-03-07T22:39:37.659Z\",\"dueDate\":null,\"slaStartedAt\":null,\"slaMediumRiskAt\":null,\"slaHighRiskAt\":null,\"slaBreachesAt\":null,\"slaType\":\"all\",\"status\":\"Done\",\"labels\":[],\"createdBy\":\"Chill\",\"createdById\":\"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7\",\"assignee\":\"Chill\",\"assigneeId\":\"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7\",\"project\":\"Hey Chill\",\"projectId\":\"5c39b383-018d-4b3b-97c7-d9a5129c119d\",\"team\":\"Forge\",\"teamId\":\"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd\"},{\"id\":\"ea9a6b42-b9e5-4ec3-bb58-c1f15e584b2f\",\"identifier\":\"FRG-214\",\"title\":\"Brainstorm post-processing: readable summary from raw transcript\",\"description\":\"After brainstorm recording stops, process the raw transcript into a clean readable format. Reference Brainwave repo for the summarization approach. Show both raw and processed versions. Send the processed version to Slack.\",\"projectMilestone\":{\"id\":\"8151d407-d1e0-4674-8fbe-d243e5be4895\",\"name\":\"M3: Daily driver \u2014 dual mode\"},\"priority\":{\"value\":2,\"name\":\"High\"},\"url\":\"https://linear.app/chills-linear/issue/FRG-214/brainstorm-post-processing-readable-summary-from-raw-transcript\",\"gitBranchName\":\"chillkimtest/frg-214-brainstorm-post-processing-readable-summary-from-raw\",\"createdAt\":\"2026-03-07T01:15:44.498Z\",\"updatedAt\":\"2026-03-07T01:15:44.498Z\",\"archivedAt\":null,\"completedAt\":null,\"dueDate\":null,\"slaStartedAt\":null,\"slaMediumRiskAt\":null,\"slaHighRiskAt\":null,\"slaBreachesAt\":null,\"slaType\":\"all\",\"status\":\"Backlog\",\"labels\":[],\"createdBy\":\"Chill\",\"createdById\":\"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7\",\"project\":\"Hey Chill\",\"projectId\":\"5c39b383-018d-4b3b-97c7-d9a5129c119d\",\"team\":\"Forge\",\"teamId\":\"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd\"},{\"id\":\"bb9b7691-c6f9-4753-8e88-bf8cc3c2aaf6\",\"identifier\":\"FRG-213\",\"title\":\"Language support: Cantonese and English\",\"description\":\"Transcription must handle both Cantonese and English speech. Auto-detect or allow user to select language. Web Speech API supports zh-HK for Cantonese. Test with mixed-language input.\",\"projectMilestone\":{\"id\":\"8151d407-d1e0-4674-8fbe-d243e5be4895\",\"name\":\"M3: Daily driver \u2014 dual mode\"},\"priority\":{\"value\":2,\"name\":\"High\"},\"url\":\"https://linear.app/chills-linear/issue/FRG-213/language-support-cantonese-and-english\",\"gitBranchName\":\"chillkimtest/frg-213-language-support-cantonese-and-english\",\"createdAt\":\"2026-03-07T01:15:44.254Z\",\"updatedAt\":\"2026-03-07T01:15:44.254Z\",\"archivedAt\":null,\"completedAt\":null,\"dueDate\":null,\"slaStartedAt\":null,\"slaMediumRiskAt\":null,\"slaHighRiskAt\":null,\"slaBreachesAt\":null,\"slaType\":\"all\",\"status\":\"Backlog\",\"labels\":[],\"createdBy\":\"Chill\",\"createdById\":\"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7\",\"project\":\"Hey Chill\",\"projectId\":\"5c39b383-018d-4b3b-97c7-d9a5129c119d\",\"team\":\"Forge\",\"teamId\":\"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd\"},{\"id\":\"4e278ef1-9dac-4b12-9f9a-07e98595f024\",\"identifier\":\"FRG-212\",\"title\":\"Brainstorm mode: continuous recording with real-time transcript\",\"description\":\"No VAD in brainstorm mode \u2014 record continuously until Stop is pressed. Show real-time transcript while speaking (reference Brainwave repo for implementation). On stop, produce both raw transcript and a cleaned readable version.\",\"projectMilestone\":{\"id\":\"8151d407-d1e0-4674-8fbe-d243e5be4895\",\"name\":\"M3: Daily driver \u2014 dual mode\"},\"priority\":{\"value\":2,\"name\":\"High\"},\"url\":\"https://linear.app/chills-linear/issue/FRG-212/brainstorm-mode-continuous-recording-with-real-time-transcript\",\"gitBranchName\":\"chillkimtest/frg-212-brainstorm-mode-continuous-recording-with-real-time\",\"createdAt\":\"2026-03-07T01:15:44.002Z\",\"updatedAt\":\"2026-03-07T01:15:44.002Z\",\"archivedAt\":null,\"completedAt\":null,\"dueDate\":null,\"slaStartedAt\":null,\"slaMediumRiskAt\":null,\"slaHighRiskAt\":null,\"slaBreachesAt\":null,\"slaType\":\"all\",\"status\":\"Backlog\",\"labels\":[],\"createdBy\":\"Chill\",\"createdById\":\"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7\",\"project\":\"Hey Chill\",\"projectId\":\"5c39b383-018d-4b3b-97c7-d9a5129c119d\",\"team\":\"Forge\",\"teamId\":\"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd\"},{\"id\":\"b3acd63c-9d87-4000-8190-74646fe3767e\",\"identifier\":\"FRG-211\",\"title\":\"Dual-mode UI: Quick Request (A) + Brainstorm (B) buttons\",\"description\":\"Replace single mic button with two distinct buttons. Button A = Quick Request (current push-to-talk with VAD). Button B = Brainstorm (continuous recording, manual stop). Clear visual distinction between modes.\",\"projectMilestone\":{\"id\":\"8151d407-d1e0-4674-8fbe-d243e5be4895\",\"name\":\"M3: Daily driver \u2014 dual mode\"},\"priority\":{\"value\":2,\"name\":\"High\"},\"url\":\"https://linear.app/chills-linear/issue/FRG-211/dual-mode-ui-quick-request-a-brainstorm-b-buttons\",\"gitBranchName\":\"chillkimtest/frg-211-dual-mode-ui-quick-request-a-brainstorm-b-buttons\",\"createdAt\":\"2026-03-07T01:15:43.753Z\",\"updatedAt\":\"2026-03-07T01:15:43.753Z\",\"archivedAt\":null,\"completedAt\":null,\"dueDate\":null,\"slaStartedAt\":null,\"slaMediumRiskAt\":null,\"slaHighRiskAt\":null,\"slaBreachesAt\":null,\"slaType\":\"all\",\"status\":\"Backlog\",\"labels\":[],\"createdBy\":\"Chill\",\"createdById\":\"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7\",\"project\":\"Hey requirement). Deploy via Docker on home server behind Cloudflare tunnel.\\n\\nAcceptance: Kim can access Brainwave at a URL and test voice brainstorming.\",\"projectMilestone\":{\"id\":\"fa68e282-c449-402b-b1dc-c64e4a35d70e\",\"name\":\"M1: Voice-to-Slack works\"},\"priority\":{\"value\":2,\"name\":\"High\"},\"url\":\"https://linear.app/chills-linear/issue/FRG-189/deploy-brainwave-for-kim-to-evaluate\",\"gitBranchName\":\"chillkimtest/frg-189-deploy-brainwave-for-kim-to-evaluate\",\"createdAt\":\"2026-03-06T04:29:08.401Z\",\"updatedAt\":\"2026-03-06T04:41:28.941Z\",\"archivedAt\":null,\"completedAt\":\"2026-03-06T04:41:28.924Z\",\"dueDate\":null,\"slaStartedAt\":null,\"slaMediumRiskAt\":null,\"slaHighRiskAt\":null,\"slaBreachesAt\":null,\"slaType\":\"all\",\"status\":\"Done\",\"labels\":[],\"createdBy\":\"Chill\",\"createdById\":\"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7\",\"project\":\"Hey Chill\",\"projectId\":\"5c39b383-018d-4b3b-97c7-d9a5129c119d\",\"team\":\"Forge\",\"teamId\":\"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd\"},{\"id\":\"95c1bfc6-dcb0-4cf5-8805-2d818fa61b7b\",\"identifier\":\"FRG-187\",\"title\":\"Push-to-talk: capture voice and transcribe\",\"description\":\"Tap the mic button \u2192 record audio \u2192 transcribe via Web Speech API or OpenAI Whisper.\\n\\nDisplay transcription on screen. hey-chill PWA repo\",\"description\":\"Create chillkimtest-oss/hey-chill repo.\\n\\nMinimal PWA scaffold:\\n\\n* index.html with mic button\\n* manifest.json for installability\\n* Service worker for offline\\n* Deploy to Vercel\\n\\nAcceptance: installable PWA loads on mobile/desktop with a visible mic button.\",\"projectMilestone\":{\"id\":\"fa68e282-c449-402b-b1dc-c64e4a35d70e\",\"name\":\"M1: Voice-to-Slack works\"},\"priority\":{\"value\":1,\"name\":\"Urgent\"},\"url\":\"https://linear.app/chills-linear/issue/FRG-186/scaffold-hey-chill-pwa-repo\",\"gitBranchName\":\"chillkimtest/frg-186-scaffold-hey-chill-pwa-repo\",\"createdAt\":\"2026-03-06T04:29:07.653Z\",\"updatedAt\":\"2026-03-06T04:41:28.202Z\",\"archivedAt\":null,\"completedAt\":\"2026-03-06T04:41:28.099Z\",\"dueDate\":null,\"slaStartedAt\":null,\"slaMediumRiskAt\":null,\"slaHighRiskAt\":null,\"slaBreachesAt\":null,\"slaType\":\"all\",\"status\":\"Done\",\"labels\":[],\"createdBy\":\"Chill\",\"createdById\":\"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7\",\"project\":\"Hey Chill\",\"projectId\":\"5c39b383-018d-4b3b-97c7-d9a5129c119d\",\"team\":\"Forge\",\"teamId\":\"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd\"},{\"id\":\"c7af639a-39e7-48ef-885a-2edfaf79f921\",\"identifier\":\"FRG-173\",\"title\":\"Wire voice commands to OpenClaw gateway for agent routing\",\"description\":\"## Context\\n\\nAfter wake word detection, transcribed voice commands need to reach OpenClaw which routes to Chill (daily ops, calendar, ideas) or other agents.\\n\\n## Requirements\\n\\n* Kuma sends transcribed text to OpenClaw chat completions endpoint\\n* OpenClaw handles routing to the right agent\\n* Response text fed to ElevenLabs TTS for spoken reply\\n* Depends on: wake word detection ticket + FRG-159 (gateway networking)\",\"projectMilestone\":{\"id\":\"fa68e282-c449-402b-b1dc-c64e4a35d70e\",\"name\":\"M1: Voice-to-Slack works\"},\"priority\":{\"value\":2,\"name\":\"High\"},\"url\":\"https://linear.app/chills-linear/issue/FRG-173/wire-voice-commands-to-openclaw-gateway-for-agent-routing\",\"gitBranchName\":\"chillkimtest/frg-173-wire-voice-commands-to-openclaw-gateway-for-agent-routing\",\"createdAt\":\"2026-03-03T06:34:07.533Z\",\"updatedAt\":\"2026-03-06T04:28:46.547Z\",\"archivedAt\":null,\"completedAt\":null,\"dueDate\":null,\"slaStartedAt\":null,\"slaMediumRiskAt\":null,\"slaHighRiskAt\":null,\"slaBreachesAt\":null,\"slaType\":\"all\",\"status\":\"Canceled\",\"labels\":[],\"createdBy\":\"Chill\",\"createdById\":\"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7\",\"project\":\"Hey Chill\",\"projectId\":\"5c39b383-018d-4b3b-97c7-d9a5129c119d\",\"team\":\"Forge\",\"teamId\":\"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd\"},{\"id\":\"dfc014ba-53f1-469d-adf6-2cf72cec2c12\",\"identifier\":\"FRG-172\",\"title\":\"Implement wake word detection (\\\"Hey Kuma\\\") via Web Speech API continuous mode\",\"description\":\"## Context\\n\\nKuma (AIRI fork) already has VAD, STT, continuous listening, and ElevenLabs TTS. Missing piece: always-on wake word detection.\\n\\n## Approach (Phase 1)\\n\\nUse Web Speech API continuous mode (already in AIRI) + string-match for 'Hey Kuma' prefix. Strip prefix, send rest to OpenClaw as voice command. Zero new deps.\\n\\n## Phase 2 (future)\\n\\nUpgrade to Picovoice Porcupine for lower battery drain and better accuracy (free tier: 1 user, 1 custom \u2026 (truncated, use `get_issue` for full description)\",\"projectMilestone\":{\"id\":\"fa68e282-c449-402b-b1dc-c64e4a35d70e\",\"name\":\"M1: Voice-to-Slack works\"},\"priority\":{\"value\":2,\"name\":\"High\"},\"url\":\"https://linear.app/chills-linear/issue/FRG-172/implement-wake-word-detection-hey-kuma-via-web-speech-api-continuous\",\"gitBranchName\":\"chillkimtest/frg-172-implement-wake-word-detection-hey-kuma-via-web-speech-api\",\"createdAt\":\"2026-03-03T06:34:07.304Z\",\"updatedAt\":\"2026-03-06T04:28:46.351Z\",\"archivedAt\":null,\"completedAt\":null,\"dueDate\":null,\"slaStartedAt\":null,\"slaMediumRiskAt\":null,\"slaHighRiskAt\":null,\"slaBreachesAt\":null,\"slaType\":\"all\",\"status\":\"Canceled\",\"labels\":[],\"createdBy\":\"Chill\",\"createdById\":\"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7\",\"project\":\"Hey Chill\",\"projectId\":\"5c39b383-018d-4b3b-97c7-d9a5129c119d\",\"team\":\"Forge\",\"teamId\":\"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd\",\"cycleId\":\"5c019f03-3dc2-4ef1-9b52-2ab2b6f9dba4\"},{\"id\":\"c336fd33-3782-4c85-adf0-3b20bd3b0800\",\"identifier\":\"FRG-159\",\"title\":\"Expose OpenClaw gateway for AIRI web app (networking/CORS)\",\"description\":\"## Problem\\n\\nThe AIRI web app ([https://airi-deploy.vercel.app](<https://airi-deploy.vercel.app>)) runs on HTTPS but the OpenClaw gateway runs on HTTP localhost. Browsers block mixed content (HTTPS\u2192HTTP). Also the gateway lacks CORS headers and a /v1/models endpoint.\\n\\n## What Worked (hack)\\n\\nA Node.js CORS proxy on port 18790 that:\\n\\n* Adds CORS headers to all responses\\n* Serves a fake /v1/models response\\n* Proxies /v1/chat/completions to the gatew\u2026 (truncated, use `get_issue` for full description)\",\"priority\":{\"value\":2,\"name\":\"High\"},\"url\":\"https://linear.app/chills-linear/issue/FRG-159/expose-openclaw-gateway-for-airi-web-app-networkingcors\",\"gitBranchName\":\"chillkimtest/frg-159-expose-openclaw-gateway-for-airi-web-app-networkingcors\",\"createdAt\":\"2026-03-01T05:13:04.918Z\",\"updatedAt\":\"2026-03-04T03:49:13.157Z\",\"archivedAt\":null,\"completedAt\":\"2026-03-03T05:55:39.351Z\",\"dueDate\":null,\"slaStartedAt\":null,\"slaMediumRiskAt\":null,\"slaHighRiskAt\":null,\"slaBreachesAt\":null,\"slaType\":\"all\",\"status\":\"Done\",\"labels\":[],\"createdBy\":\"Chill\",\"createdById\":\"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7\",\"assignee\":\"kimwong.wwk@gmail.com\",\"assigneeId\":\"fd40b97e-c958-48cb-8745-e5e9f9ac768f\",\"project\":\"Hey Chill\",\"projectId\":\"5c39b383-018d-4b3b-97c7-d9a5129c119d\",\"team\":\"Forge\",\"teamId\":\"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd\"},{\"id\":\"f24f9f80-e121-4f0f-a97a-cd091e514b76\",\"identifier\":\"FRG-157\",\"title\":\"Configure Chill character appearance in AIRI\",\"description\":\"Appearance only \u2014 personality handled by OpenClaw (FRG-160 done).\\n\\nOptions for Chill avatar:\\n\\n1. Use bundled AvatarSample_A/B.vrm (quick, generic)\\n2. Download free VRM from VRoid Hub ([hub.vroid.com](<http://hub.vroid.com>)) or [Booth.pm](<http://Booth.pm>) (\\\\~1884 free VRM models on Booth)\\n3. Create custom in VRoid Studio (free, best result, needs GUI)\\n\\nAIRI character config: Settings > Characters > Create. Supports VRM and Live2D.\\n\\nKey files: \u2026 (truncated, use `get_issue` for full description)\",\"priority\":{\"value\":3,\"name\":\"Medium\"},\"url\":\"https://linear.app/chills-linear/issue/FRG-157/configure-chill-character-appearance-in-airi\",\"gitBranchName\":\"chillkimtest/frg-157-configure-chill-character-appearance-in-airi\",\"createdAt\":\"2026-03-01T03:30:58.758Z\",\"updatedAt\":\"2026-03-03T06:24:50.030Z\",\"archivedAt\":null,\"completedAt\":null,\"dueDate\":null,\"slaStartedAt\":null,\"slaMediumRiskAt\":null,\"slaHighRiskAt\":null,\"slaBreachesAt\":null,\"slaType\":\"all\",\"status\":\"Canceled\",\"labels\":[],\"createdBy\":\"Chill\",\"createdById\":\"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7\",\"project\":\"Hey Chill\",\"projectId\":\"5c39b383-018d-4b3b-97c7-d9a5129c119d\",\"team\":\"Forge\",\"teamId\":\"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd\"},{\"id\":\"b3ce30c6-bdee-4f4c-9469-5d7ee33a8a68\",\"identifier\":\"FRG-156\",\"title\":\"Build and package Electron desktop app (stage-tamagotchi)\",\"description\":\"Cannot build in DevLab (4GB Docker OOM) or on Forge's machine. AIRI monorepo pnpm install alone exceeds 4GB. Needs a machine with 8GB+ RAM.\\n\\nBlocked until we have a larger build machine or find a way to build stage-tamagotchi in isolation.\",\"priority\":{\"value\":3,\"name\":\"Medium\"},\"url\":\"https://linear.app/chills-linear/issue/FRG-156/build-and-package-electron-desktop-app-stage-tamagotchi\",\"gitBranchName\":\"chillkimtest/frg-156-build-and-package-electron-desktop-app-stage-tamagotchi\",\"createdAt\":\"2026-03-01T03:30:58.506Z\",\"updatedAt\":\"2026-03-03T05:56:17.180Z\",\"archivedAt\":null,\"completedAt\":null,\"dueDate\":null,\"slaStartedAt\":null,\"slaMediumRiskAt\":null,\"slaHighRiskAt\":null,\"slaBreachesAt\":null,\"slaType\":\"all\",\"status\":\"Canceled\",\"labels\":[\"blocked\"],\"createdBy\":\"Chill\",\"createdById\":\"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7\",\"project\":\"Hey Chill\",\"projectId\":\"5c39b383-018d-4b3b-97c7-d9a5129c119d\",\"team\":\"Forge\",\"teamId\":\"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd\"},{\"id\":\"7dd90345-efc8-4a2c-b66d-0e5b4847f697\",\"identifier\":\"FRG-163\",\"title\":\"Fix emotion-to-motion mapping for default Live2D model\",\"description\":\"LLM emits ACT emotion tags but mapping targets non-existent motion groups. Only neutral\u2192Idle works. Need to remap to actual model groups (Idle, Flick, FlickDown, FlickUp, Tap, Tap@Body, Flick@Body) and add direct motion field support.\",\"priority\":{\"value\":1,\"name\":\"Urgent\"},\"url\":\"https://linear.app/chills-linear/issue/FRG-163/fix-emotion-to-motion-mapping-for-default-live2d-model\",\"gitBranchName\":\"chillkimtest/frg-163-fix-emotion-to-motion-mapping-for-default-live2d-model\",\"createdAt\":\"2026-03-01T21:04:56.514Z\",\"updatedAt\":\"2026-03-01T21:09:37.404Z\",\"archivedAt\":null,\"completedAt\":\"2026-03-01T21:09:37.385Z\",\"dueDate\":null,\"slaStartedAt\":null,\"slaMediumRiskAt\":null,\"slaHighRiskAt\":null,\"slaBreachesAt\":null,\"slaType\":\"all\",\"status\":\"Done\",\"labels\":[],\"createdBy\":\"Chill\",\"createdById\":\"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7\",\"project\":\"Hey Chill\",\"projectId\":\"5c39b383-018d-4b3b-97c7-d9a5129c119d\",\"team\":\"Forge\",\"teamId\":\"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd\"},{\"id\":\"48e8991c-32c5-42b4-8ae7-7ddef1bd2cf0\",\"identifier\":\"FRG-160\",\"title\":\"OpenClaw provider: skip AIRI character system prompt\",\"description\":\"AIRI prepends its own character personality prompt to messages sent to the LLM. When using OpenClaw as the provider, this overrides the agent identity defined in [SOUL.md/IDENTITY.md](<http://SOUL.md/IDENTITY.md>) \u2014 so the agent thinks it's Airi instead of Chill.\\n\\nFix: In the OpenClaw provider's createProvider or streamFrom path, strip/skip the AIRI character system prompt. Let OpenClaw's gateway handle identity entirely.\\n\\nKey files:\\n\\n* packages\u2026 (truncated, use `get_issue` for full description)\",\"priority\":{\"value\":1,\"name\":\"Urgent\"},\"url\":\"https://linear.app/chills-linear/issue/FRG-160/openclaw-provider-skip-airi-character-system-prompt\",\"gitBranchName\":\"chillkimtest/frg-160-openclaw-provider-skip-airi-character-system-prompt\",\"createdAt\":\"2026-03-01T18:03:53.697Z\",\"updatedAt\":\"2026-03-01T19:19:02.400Z\",\"archivedAt\":null,\"completedAt\":\"2026-03-01T19:19:02.384Z\",\"dueDate\":null,\"slaStartedAt\":null,\"slaMediumRiskAt\":null,\"slaHighRiskAt\":null,\"slaBreachesAt\":null,\"slaType\":\"all\",\"status\":\"Done\",\"labels\":[],\"createdBy\":\"Chill\",\"createdById\":\"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7\",\"project\":\"Hey Chill\",\"projectId\":\"5c39b383-018d-4b3b-97c7-d9a5129c119d\",\"team\":\"Forge\",\"teamId\":\"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd\"},{\"id\":\"a614623d-5774-46dd-87b2-986f5be847fb\",\"identifier\":\"FRG-162\",\"title\":\"Connect AIRI repo to Vercel for auto-deploy on push\",\"description\":\"Vercel needs a GitHub Login Connection to enable auto-deploy.\\n\\nSteps:\\n\\n1. Go to [https://vercel.com/account/login-connections](<https://vercel.com/account/login-connections>)\\n2. Add GitHub connection (OAuth)\\n3. Go to [https://vercel.com/chills-projects-19d98ccf/airi-deploy/settings/git](<https://vercel.com/chills-projects-19d98ccf/airi-deploy/settings/git>)\\n4. Connect repo: chillkimtest-oss/airi\\n5. OpenClaw gateway doesn't serve this endpoint.\\n\\nOptions:\\n\\n1. Let user type model name manually (e.g. openclaw:chill) \u2014 skip model discovery\\n2. Add a config field for model name in the provider settings\\n3. Make sure CI works and all tests pass I need to pull the full FRG-237 issue details to see what QA work was previously done, then examine the actual Hey Chill app to understand its current state before setting up the Playwright infrastructure and writing comprehensive tests.

{"id":"207a955d-7044-4ef7-bb0a-b6b712180f56","identifier":"FRG-237","title":"Set up QA regression suite for Hey Chill","description":"## Context

Hey Chill M1 is complete. Before M2 starts, we need automated regression tests.

## What to do

1. Read the project description in Linear — it contains the cumulative feature spec.
2. Set up testing in the main repo (chillkimtest-oss/hey-chill). Playwright recommended for PWA testing.
3. Write regression tests covering: push-to-talk UI, VAD silence timeout (3.5s), transcription display, Slack webhook send, PWA installability, service worker.
4. Tests run against: [https://brainwave-ten.vercel.app](<https://brainwave-ten.vercel.app>)

## Key Constraints

* Web Speech API may not work in headless Chrome — consider how to test transcription. May need to mock the SpeechRecognition API.
* Webhook send test: verify the fetch call is made with correct payload. May need to intercept network requests rather than hit the real webhook.
* PWA tests: verify manifest, service worker registration, installability criteria.

## Acceptance Criteria

* Test suite covers all features listed in the project description
* Tests pass against current deployment
* README explains how to run tests","priority":{"value":2,"name":"High"},"url":"https://linear.app/chills-linear/issue/FRG-237/set-up-qa-regression-suite-for-hey-chill","gitBranchName":"chillkimtest/frg-237-set-up-qa-regression-suite-for-hey-chill","createdAt":"2026-03-07T23:22:09.633Z","updatedAt":"2026-03-08T02:29:03.513Z","archivedAt":null,"completedAt":"2026-03-08T02:29:03.494Z","dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"Done","labels":["testing"],"attachments":[],"documents":[],"createdBy":"Chill","createdById":"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7","project":"Hey Chill","projectId":"5c39b383-018d-4b3b-97c7-d9a5129c119d","team":"Forge","teamId":"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd"}

Set up testing in the main repo (chillkimtest-oss/hey-chill). Playwright recommended for PWA testing.\\n3. Let me look at the actual deployed app first to understand what it has, and then I need to create a complete Playwright test suite in this repo. The URL for testing is mentioned as: - https://brainwave-ten.vercel.app (in the issue) - https://hey-chill.vercel.app (in the task instructions) Let me visit the actual app to understand its structure. I see now—the task instructions describe the end goal of 54 passing tests, but the repo is currently empty, so that's what I need to build. I'll start by examining the actual PWA at https://hey-chill.vercel.app to understand its features, then set up Playwright from scratch and write comprehensive regression tests.

# evaluate_script response
Script ran on page and returned:
```json
{"title":"Hey Chill","html":"<html lang=\"en\"><head>
  <meta charset=\"UTF-8\">
  <meta name=\"viewport\" content=\"width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0, viewport-fit=cover\">
  <meta name=\"theme-color\" content=\"#1a1a2e\">
  <meta name=\"description\" content=\"Voice-first AI agent — Hey Chill\">
  <meta name=\"mobile-web-app-capable\" content=\"yes\">
  <meta name=\"apple-mobile-web-app-capable\" content=\"yes\">
  <meta name=\"apple-mobile-web-app-status-bar-style\" content=\"black-translucent\">
  <meta name=\"apple-mobile-web-app-title\" content=\"Chill\">
  <link rel=\"manifest\" href=\"manifest.json\">
  <link rel=\"icon\" href=\"icons/icon-192.svg\" type=\"image/svg+xml\">
  <link rel=\"apple-touch-icon\" href=\"icons/icon-192.svg\">
  <link rel=\"stylesheet\" href=\"style.css\">
  <title>Hey Chill</title>
</head>
<body>
  <div id=\"app\">
    <!-- Header -->
    <header class=\"header\">
      <div class=\"header-title\">
        <span class=\"logo-dot\"></span>
        <h1>Chill</h1>
      </div>
      <button class=\"icon-btn\" id=\"settings-btn\" aria-label=\"Settings\" title=\"Settings\">
        <svg viewBox=\"0 0 24 24\" fill=\"none\" stroke=\"currentColor\" stroke-width=\"2\" stroke-linecap=\"round\" stroke-linejoin=\"round\">
          <circle cx=\"12\" cy=\"12\" r=\"3\"></circle>
          <path d=\"M19.4 15a1.65 1.65 0 0 0 .33 1.82l.06.06a2 2 0 0 1-2.83 2.83l-.06-.06a1.65 1.65 0 0 0-1.82-.33 1.65 1.65 0 0 0-1 1.51V21a2 2 0 0 1-4 0v-.09A1.65 1.65 0 0 0 9 19.4a1.65 1.65 0 0 0-1.82.33l-.06.06a2 2 0 0 1-2.83-2.83l.06-.06A1.65 1.65 0 0 0 4.68 15a1.65 1.65 0 0 0-1.51-1H3a2 2 0 0 1 0-4h.09A1.65 1.65 0 0 0 4.6 9a1.65 1.65 0 0 0-.33-1.82l-.06-.06a2 2 0 0 1 2.83-2.83l.06.06A1.65 1.65 0 0 0 9 4.68a1.65 1.65 0 0 0 1-1.51V3a2 2 0 0 1 4 0v.09a1.65 1.65 0 0 0 1 1.51 1.65 1.65 0 0 0 1.82-.33l.06-.06a2 2 0 0 1 2.83 2.83l-.06.06A1.65 1.65 0 0 0 19.4 9a1.65 1.65 0 0 0 1.51 1H21a2 2 0 0 1 0 4h-.09a1.65 1.65 0 0 0-1.51 1z\"></path>
        </svg>
      </button>
    </header>

    <!-- Message history -->
    <main class=\"messages\" id=\"messages\" aria-live=\"polite\" aria-label=\"Message history\">
      <div class=\"messages-empty\" id=\"messages-empty\">
        <p>Your messages will appear here</p>
      </div>
    </main>

    <!-- Transcript preview (shown after speaking) -->
    <div class=\"transcript-bar\" id=\"transcript-bar\" hidden=\"\">
      <p class=\"transcript-text\" id=\"transcript-text\"></p>
      <div class=\"transcript-actions\">
        <button class=\"btn-ghost\" id=\"discard-btn\">Discard</button>
        <button class=\"btn-send\" id=\"send-btn\">Send</button>
      </div>
    </div>

    <!-- Live transcript (shown while listening) -->
    <div class=\"live-transcript\" id=\"live-transcript\" hidden=\"\">
      <p id=\"live-text\">Listening…</p>
    </div>

    <!-- Mic area -->
    <section class=\"mic-section\">
      <div class=\"mic-hint\" id=\"mic-hint\">Tap to speak</div>
      <div class=\"mic-ring\" id=\"mic-ring\">
        <button class=\"mic-btn\" id=\"mic-btn\" aria-label=\"Start recording\" aria-pressed=\"false\">
          <svg class=\"mic-icon\" viewBox=\"0 0 24 24\" fill=\"currentColor\">
            <path d=\"M12 1a4 4 0 0 1 4 4v7a4 4 0 0 1-8 0V5a4 4 0 0 1 4-4z\"></path>
            <path d=\"M19 11a1 1 0 0 1 1 1 8 8 0 0 1-16 0 1 1 0 1 1 2 0 6 6 0 0 0 12 0 1 1 0 0 1 1-1z\"></path>
            <line x1=\"12\" y1=\"19\" x2=\"12\" y2=\"23\" stroke=\"currentColor\" stroke-width=\"2\" stroke-linecap=\"round\"></line>
            <line x1=\"8\" y1=\"23\" x2=\"16\" y2=\"23\" stroke=\"currentColor\" stroke-width=\"2\" stroke-linecap=\"round\"></line>
          </svg>
        </button>
      </div>
    </section>

    <!-- Not supported banner -->
    <div class=\"not-supported\" id=\"not-supported\" hidden=\"\">
      <p>Speech recognition is not supported in this browser. Try Chrome or Edge.</p>
    </div>
  </div>

  <!-- Settings modal -->
  <div class=\"modal-overlay\" id=\"modal-overlay\">
    <div class=\"modal\" role=\"dialog\" aria-modal=\"true\" aria-labelledby=\"modal-title\">
      <div class=\"modal-header\">
        <h2 id=\"modal-title\">Settings</h2>
        <button class=\"icon-btn\" id=\"modal-close\" aria-label=\"Close settings\">
          <svg viewBox=\"0 0 24 24\" fill=\"none\" stroke=\"currentColor\" stroke-width=\"2\" stroke-linecap=\"round\" stroke-linejoin=\"round\">
            <line x1=\"18\" y1=\"6\" x2=\"6\" y2=\"18\"></line>
            <line x1=\"6\" y1=\"6\" x2=\"18\" y2=\"18\"></line>
          </svg>
        </button>
      </div>
      <div class=\"modal-body\">
        <label for=\"webhook-url\">Webhook URL</label>
        <input type=\"url\" id=\"webhook-url\" placeholder=\"https://your-webhook.example.com/hook\" autocomplete=\"off\" spellcheck=\"false\">
        <p class=\"field-hint\">Messages will be POSTed here as JSON.</p>
      </div>
      <div class=\"modal-footer\">
        <button class=\"btn-send\" id=\"save-settings\">Save</button>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>

  <script src=\"app.js\"></script>


</body></html>"}
```

USERMESSAGE #42
✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
# evaluate_script response
Script ran on page and returned:
```json
"'use strict';

/* ===== DOM refs ===== */
const micBtn        = document.getElementById('mic-btn');
const micRing       = document.getElementById('mic-ring');
const micHint       = document.getElementById('mic-hint');
const liveTranscript = document.getElementById('live-transcript');
const liveText      = document.getElementById('live-text');
const transcriptBar = document.getElementById('transcript-bar');
const transcriptText = document.getElementById('transcript-text');
const sendBtn       = document.getElementById('send-btn');
const discardBtn    = document.getElementById('discard-btn');
const messagesEl    = document.getElementById('messages');
const messagesEmpty = document.getElementById('messages-empty');
const notSupported  = document.getElementById('not-supported');
const settingsBtn   = document.getElementById('settings-btn');
const modalOverlay  = document.getElementById('modal-overlay');
const modalClose    = document.getElementById('modal-close');
const webhookInput  = document.getElementById('webhook-url');
const saveSettings  = document.getElementById('save-settings');

/* ===== State ===== */
let recognition     = null;
let isListening     = false;
let finalTranscript = '';
let interimText     = '';
let silenceTimer    = null;
const SILENCE_DELAY = 3500; // ms of silence before auto-stop

/* ===== Service Worker ===== */
if ('serviceWorker' in navigator) {
  navigator.serviceWorker.register('sw.js').catch(() => {/* silent */});
}

/* ===== Speech Recognition setup ===== */
const SpeechRecognition = window.SpeechRecognition || window.webkitSpeechRecognition;

if (!SpeechRecognition) {
  notSupported.hidden = false;
  micBtn.disabled = true;
  micBtn.setAttribute('aria-disabled', 'true');
} else {
  initRecognition();
}

function initRecognition() {
  recognition = new SpeechRecognition();
  recognition.continuous     = true;
  recognition.interimResults = true;
  recognition.lang           = navigator.language || 'en-US';
  recognition.maxAlternatives = 1;

  recognition.onstart = () => {
    isListening = true;
    finalTranscript = '';
    interimText = '';
    setRecordingUI(true);
  };

  recognition.onresult = (event) => {
    let interim = '';
    for (let i = event.resultIndex; i < event.results.length; i++) {
      const result = event.results[i];
      if (result.isFinal) {
        finalTranscript += result[0].transcript + ' ';
      } else {
        interim += result[0].transcript;
      }
    }
    interimText = interim;
    const displayText = (finalTranscript + interim).trim();
    updateLiveText(displayText);

    // Reset silence timer on every result
    resetSilenceTimer();
  };

  recognition.onerror = (event) => {
    // 'no-speech' is common and expected — just stop cleanly
    if (event.error !== 'no-speech') {
      console.warn('Speech recognition error:', event.error);
    }
    stopListening();
  };

  recognition.onend = () => {
    // onend fires when recognition stops for any reason
    if (isListening) {
      // We didn't call stop manually yet; auto-stop happened
      finishRecording();
    }
  };
}

function resetSilenceTimer() {
  clearTimeout(silenceTimer);
  silenceTimer = setTimeout(() => {
    if (isListening) stopListening();
  }, SILENCE_DELAY);
}

/* ===== Recording controls ===== */
function startListening() {
  if (!recognition || isListening) return;
  hideTranscriptBar();

  try {
    recognition.start();
  } catch (e) {
    console.warn('Could not start recognition:', e);
    // Re-init in case of invalid state
    initRecognition();
    try { recognition.start(); } catch (_) {}
  }

  resetSilenceTimer();
}

function stopListening() {
  clearTimeout(silenceTimer);
  isListening = false; // mark before calling stop so onend doesn't re-trigger finishRecording
  if (recognition) {
    try { recognition.stop(); } catch (_) {}
  }
  finishRecording();
}

function finishRecording() {
  setRecordingUI(false);
  const text = finalTranscript.trim();
  if (text) {
    showTranscriptBar(text);
  }
}

/* ===== UI state helpers ===== */
function setRecordingUI(recording) {
  micBtn.classList.toggle('recording', recording);
  micRing.classList.toggle('recording', recording);
  micBtn.setAttribute('aria-pressed', recording ? 'true' : 'false');
  micBtn.setAttribute('aria-label', recording ? 'Stop recording' : 'Start recording');
  micHint.textContent = recording ? 'Tap to stop' : 'Tap to speak';
  micHint.classList.toggle('active', recording);
  liveTranscript.hidden = !recording;
  if (!recording) {
    liveText.textContent = 'Listening…';
    liveText.classList.remove('has-text');
  }
}

function updateLiveText(text) {
  if (text) {
    liveText.textContent = text;
    liveText.classList.add('has-text');
  } else {
    liveText.textContent = 'Listening…';
    liveText.classList.remove('has-text');
  }
}

function showTranscriptBar(text) {
  transcriptText.textContent = text;
  transcriptBar.hidden = false;
}

function hideTranscriptBar() {
  transcriptBar.hidden = true;
  transcriptText.textContent = '';
}

/* ===== Mic button click (toggle) ===== */
micBtn.addEventListener('click', () => {
  if (isListening) {
    stopListening();
  } else {
    startListening();
  }
});

/* ===== Send / Discard ===== */
sendBtn.addEventListener('click', async () => {
  const text = transcriptText.textContent.trim();
  if (!text) return;
  hideTranscriptBar();
  await sendMessage(text);
});

discardBtn.addEventListener('click', () => {
  hideTranscriptBar();
});

/* ===== Webhook send ===== */
async function sendMessage(text) {
  const webhookUrl = localStorage.getItem('webhookUrl') || '';
  const timestamp  = new Date().toISOString();
  const payload    = { text, source: 'hey-chill', timestamp };

  // Add message to history immediately
  const msgEl = addMessage(text, timestamp, 'sending');

  if (!webhookUrl) {
    updateMessageStatus(msgEl, 'no-webhook');
    return;
  }

  try {
    const res = await fetch(webhookUrl, {
      method: 'POST',
      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
      body: JSON.stringify(payload),
    });
    if (!res.ok) throw new Error(`HTTP ${res.status}`);
    updateMessageStatus(msgEl, 'sent');
  } catch (err) {
    console.warn('Webhook error:', err);
    updateMessageStatus(msgEl, 'error');
  }
}

/* ===== Message history ===== */
function addMessage(text, timestamp, status) {
  // Hide empty state
  messagesEmpty.style.display = 'none';

  const el = document.createElement('div');
  el.className = `message ${status}`;

  const timeStr = new Date(timestamp).toLocaleTimeString([], {
    hour: '2-digit',
    minute: '2-digit',
  });

  const statusLabel = {
    sending:    'Sending…',
    sent:       'Sent',
    error:      'Failed',
    'no-webhook': 'No webhook',
  };

  el.innerHTML = `
    <p class=\"message-text\">${escapeHtml(text)}</p>
    <div class=\"message-meta\">
      <span class=\"message-time\">${timeStr}</span>
      <span class=\"message-status\">${statusLabel[status] || status}</span>
    </div>
  `;

  messagesEl.appendChild(el);
  el.scrollIntoView({ behavior: 'smooth', block: 'end' });
  return el;
}

function updateMessageStatus(el, status) {
  el.className = `message ${status}`;
  const statusEl = el.querySelector('.message-status');
  const labels = {
    sent:         'Sent',
    error:        'Failed',
    'no-webhook': 'No webhook set',
  };
  if (statusEl) statusEl.textContent = labels[status] || status;
}

function escapeHtml(str) {
  return str
    .replace(/&/g, '&amp;')
    .replace(/</g, '&lt;')
    .replace(/>/g, '&gt;')
    .replace(/\"/g, '&quot;')
    .replace(/'/g, '&#39;');
}

/* ===== Settings modal ===== */
settingsBtn.addEventListener('click', openSettings);
modalClose.addEventListener('click',  closeSettings);
modalOverlay.addEventListener('click', (e) => {
  if (e.target === modalOverlay) closeSettings();
});

document.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => {
  if (e.key === 'Escape') closeSettings();
});

saveSettings.addEventListener('click', () => {
  const url = webhookInput.value.trim();
  localStorage.setItem('webhookUrl', url);
  closeSettings();
});

function openSettings() {
  webhookInput.value = localStorage.getItem('webhookUrl') || '';
  modalOverlay.hidden = false;
  webhookInput.focus();
}

function closeSettings() {
  modalOverlay.hidden = true;
}
"
```

      {
        "type": "text",
        "text": "# evaluate_script response\nScript ran on page and returned:\n```json\n\"'use strict';\\n\\n/* ===== DOM refs ===== */\\nconst micBtn        = document.getElementById('mic-btn');\\nconst micRing       = document.getElementById('mic-ring');\\nconst micHint       = document.getElementById('mic-hint');\\nconst liveTranscript = document.getElementById('live-transcript');\\nconst liveText      = document.getElementById('live-text');\\nconst transcriptBar = document.getElementById('transcript-bar');\\nconst transcriptText = document.getElementById('transcript-text');\\nconst sendBtn       = document.getElementById('send-btn');\\nconst discardBtn    = document.getElementById('discard-btn');\\nconst messagesEl    = document.getElementById('messages');\\nconst messagesEmpty = document.getElementById('messages-empty');\\nconst notSupported  = document.getElementById('not-supported');\\nconst settingsBtn   = document.getElementById('settings-btn');\\nconst modalOverlay  = document.getElementById('modal-overlay');\\nconst modalClose    = document.getElementById('modal-close');\\nconst webhookInput  = document.getElementById('webhook-url');\\nconst saveSettings  = document.getElementById('save-settings');\\n\\n/* ===== State ===== */\\nlet recognition     = null;\\nlet isListening     = false;\\nlet finalTranscript = '';\\nlet interimText     = '';\\nlet silenceTimer    = null;\\nconst SILENCE_DELAY = 3500; // ms of silence before auto-stop\\n\\n/* ===== Service Worker ===== */\\nif ('serviceWorker' in navigator) {\\n  navigator.serviceWorker.register('sw.js').catch(() => {/* silent */});\\n}\\n\\n/* ===== Speech Recognition setup ===== */\\nconst SpeechRecognition = window.SpeechRecognition || window.webkitSpeechRecognition;\\n\\nif (!SpeechRecognition) {\\n  notSupported.hidden = false;\\n  micBtn.disabled = true;\\n  micBtn.setAttribute('aria-disabled', 'true');\\n} else {\\n  initRecognition();\\n}\\n\\nfunction initRecognition() {\\n  recognition = new SpeechRecognition();\\n  recognition.continuous     = true;\\n  recognition.interimResults = true;\\n  recognition.lang           = navigator.language || 'en-US';\\n  recognition.maxAlternatives = 1;\\n\\n  recognition.onstart = () => {\\n    isListening = true;\\n    finalTranscript = '';\\n    interimText = '';\\n    setRecordingUI(true);\\n  };\\n\\n  recognition.onresult = (event) => {\\n    let interim = '';\\n    for (let i = event.resultIndex; i < event.results.length; i++) {\\n      const result = event.results[i];\\n      if (result.isFinal) {\\n        finalTranscript += result[0].transcript + ' ';\\n      } else {\\n        interim += result[0].transcript;\\n      }\\n    }\\n    interimText = interim;\\n    const displayText = (finalTranscript + interim).trim();\\n    updateLiveText(displayText);\\n\\n    // Reset silence timer on every result\\n    resetSilenceTimer();\\n  };\\n\\n  recognition.onerror = (event) => {\\n    // 'no-speech' is common and expected \u2014 just stop cleanly\\n    if (event.error !== 'no-speech') {\\n      console.warn('Speech recognition error:', event.error);\\n    }\\n    stopListening();\\n  };\\n\\n  recognition.onend = () => {\\n    // onend fires when recognition stops for any reason\\n    if (isListening) {\\n      // We didn't call stop manually yet; auto-stop happened\\n      finishRecording();\\n    }\\n  };\\n}\\n\\nfunction resetSilenceTimer() {\\n  clearTimeout(silenceTimer);\\n  silenceTimer = setTimeout(() => {\\n    if (isListening) stopListening();\\n  }, SILENCE_DELAY);\\n}\\n\\n/* ===== Recording controls ===== */\\nfunction startListening() {\\n  if (!recognition || isListening) return;\\n  hideTranscriptBar();\\n\\n  try {\\n    recognition.start();\\n  } catch (e) {\\n    console.warn('Could not start recognition:', e);\\n    // Re-init in case of invalid state\\n    initRecognition();\\n    try { recognition.start(); } catch (_) {}\\n  }\\n\\n  resetSilenceTimer();\\n}\\n\\nfunction stopListening() {\\n  clearTimeout(silenceTimer);\\n  isListening = false; // mark before calling stop so onend doesn't re-trigger finishRecording\\n  if (recognition) {\\n    try { recognition.stop(); } catch (_) {}\\n  }\\n  finishRecording();\\n}\\n\\nfunction finishRecording() {\\n  setRecordingUI(false);\\n  const text = finalTranscript.trim();\\n  if (text) {\\n    showTranscriptBar(text);\\n  }\\n}\\n\\n/* ===== UI state helpers ===== */\\nfunction setRecordingUI(recording) {\\n  micBtn.classList.toggle('recording', recording);\\n  micRing.classList.toggle('recording', recording);\\n  micBtn.setAttribute('aria-pressed', recording ? 'true' : 'false');\\n  micBtn.setAttribute('aria-label', recording ? 'Stop recording' : 'Start recording');\\n  micHint.textContent = recording ? 'Tap to stop' : 'Tap to speak';\\n  micHint.classList.toggle('active', recording);\\n  liveTranscript.hidden = !recording;\\n  if (!recording) {\\n    liveText.textContent = 'Listening\u2026';\\n    liveText.classList.remove('has-text');\\n  }\\n}\\n\\nfunction updateLiveText(text) {\\n  if (text) {\\n    liveText.textContent = text;\\n    liveText.classList.add('has-text');\\n  } else {\\n    liveText.textContent = 'Listening\u2026';\\n    liveText.classList.remove('has-text');\\n  }\\n}\\n\\nfunction showTranscriptBar(text) {\\n  transcriptText.textContent = text;\\n  transcriptBar.hidden = false;\\n}\\n\\nfunction hideTranscriptBar() {\\n  transcriptBar.hidden = true;\\n  transcriptText.textContent = '';\\n}\\n\\n/* ===== Mic button click (toggle) ===== */\\nmicBtn.addEventListener('click', () => {\\n  if (isListening) {\\n    stopListening();\\n  } else {\\n    startListening();\\n  }\\n});\\n\\n/* ===== Send / Discard ===== */\\nsendBtn.addEventListener('click', async () => {\\n  const text = transcriptText.textContent.trim();\\n  if (!text) return;\\n  hideTranscriptBar();\\n  await sendMessage(text);\\n});\\n\\ndiscardBtn.addEventListener('click', () => {\\n  hideTranscriptBar();\\n});\\n\\n/* ===== Webhook send ===== */\\nasync function sendMessage(text) {\\n  const webhookUrl = localStorage.getItem('webhookUrl') || '';\\n  const timestamp  = new Date().toISOString();\\n  const payload    = { text, source: 'hey-chill', timestamp };\\n\\n  // Add message to history immediately\\n  const msgEl = addMessage(text, timestamp, 'sending');\\n\\n  if (!webhookUrl) {\\n    updateMessageStatus(msgEl, 'no-webhook');\\n    return;\\n  }\\n\\n  try {\\n    const res = await fetch(webhookUrl, {\\n      method: 'POST',\\n      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },\\n      body: JSON.stringify(payload),\\n    });\\n    if (!res.ok) throw new Error(`HTTP ${res.status}`);\\n    updateMessageStatus(msgEl, 'sent');\\n  } catch (err) {\\n    console.warn('Webhook error:', err);\\n    updateMessageStatus(msgEl, 'error');\\n  }\\n}\\n\\n/* ===== Message history ===== */\\nfunction addMessage(text, timestamp, status) {\\n  // Hide empty state\\n  messagesEmpty.style.display = 'none';\\n\\n  const el = document.createElement('div');\\n  el.className = `message ${status}`;\\n\\n  const timeStr = new Date(timestamp).toLocaleTimeString([], {\\n    hour: '2-digit',\\n    minute: '2-digit',\\n  });\\n\\n  const statusLabel = {\\n    sending:    'Sending\u2026',\\n    sent:       'Sent',\\n    error:      'Failed',\\n    'no-webhook': 'No webhook',\\n  };\\n\\n  el.innerHTML = `\\n    <p class=\\\"message-text\\\">${escapeHtml(text)}</p>\\n    <div class=\\\"message-meta\\\">\\n      <span class=\\\"message-time\\\">${timeStr}</span>\\n      <span class=\\\"message-status\\\">${statusLabel[status] || status}</span>\\n    </div>\\n  `;\\n\\n  messagesEl.appendChild(el);\\n  el.scrollIntoView({ behavior: 'smooth', block: 'end' });\\n  return el;\\n}\\n\\nfunction updateMessageStatus(el, status) {\\n  el.className = `message ${status}`;\\n  const statusEl = el.querySelector('.message-status');\\n  const labels = {\\n    sent:         'Sent',\\n    error:        'Failed',\\n    'no-webhook': 'No webhook set',\\n  };\\n  if (statusEl) statusEl.textContent = labels[status] || status;\\n}\\n\\nfunction escapeHtml(str) {\\n  return str\\n    .replace(/&/g, '&amp;')\\n    .replace(/</g, '&lt;')\\n    .replace(/>/g, '&gt;')\\n    .replace(/\\\"/g, '&quot;')\\n    .replace(/'/g, '&#39;');\\n}\\n\\n/* ===== Settings modal ===== */\\nsettingsBtn.addEventListener('click', openSettings);\\nmodalClose.addEventListener('click',  closeSettings);\\nmodalOverlay.addEventListener('click', (e) => {\\n  if (e.target === modalOverlay) closeSettings();\\n});\\n\\ndocument.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => {\\n  if (e.key === 'Escape') closeSettings();\\n});\\n\\nsaveSettings.addEventListener('click', () => {\\n  const url = webhookInput.value.trim();\\n  localStorage.setItem('webhookUrl', url);\\n  closeSettings();\\n});\\n\\nfunction openSettings() {\\n  webhookInput.value = localStorage.getItem('webhookUrl') || '';\\n  modalOverlay.hidden = false;\\n  webhookInput.focus();\\n}\\n\\nfunction closeSettings() {\\n  modalOverlay.hidden = true;\\n}\\n\"\n```"
      }
    ]
  }
}
