USERMESSAGE #50
✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
# evaluate_script response
Script ran on page and returned:
```json
"/* Hey Chill — Service Worker */
'use strict';

const CACHE_NAME = 'hey-chill-v1';

// App shell assets to cache on install
const APP_SHELL = [
  '/',
  '/index.html',
  '/app.js',
  '/style.css',
  '/manifest.json',
  '/icons/icon-192.svg',
  '/icons/icon-512.svg',
];

/* ===== Install: pre-cache app shell ===== */
self.addEventListener('install', (event) => {
  event.waitUntil(
    caches.open(CACHE_NAME).then((cache) => cache.addAll(APP_SHELL))
  );
  // Activate immediately without waiting for tabs to close
  self.skipWaiting();
});

/* ===== Activate: clean up old caches ===== */
self.addEventListener('activate', (event) => {
  event.waitUntil(
    caches.keys().then((keys) =>
      Promise.all(
        keys
          .filter((k) => k !== CACHE_NAME)
          .map((k) => caches.delete(k))
      )
    )
  );
  // Take control of all clients immediately
  self.clients.claim();
});

/* ===== Fetch: cache-first for shell, network-first for API ===== */
self.addEventListener('fetch', (event) => {
  const { request } = event;
  const url = new URL(request.url);

  // Only handle same-origin GET requests (don't intercept webhook POSTs)
  if (request.method !== 'GET' || url.origin !== self.location.origin) {
    return;
  }

  event.respondWith(
    caches.match(request).then((cached) => {
      if (cached) return cached;

      return fetch(request).then((response) => {
        // Cache successful responses for app shell resources
        if (response.ok) {
          const clone = response.clone();
          caches.open(CACHE_NAME).then((cache) => cache.put(request, clone));
        }
        return response;
      }).catch(() => {
        // Offline fallback — return index.html for navigation requests
        if (request.destination === 'document') {
          return caches.match('/index.html');
        }
      });
    })
  );
});
"
```

shell assets to cache on install\\nconst APP_SHELL = [\\n  '/',\\n  '/index.html',\\n  '/app.js',\\n  '/style.css',\\n  '/manifest.json',\\n  '/icons/icon-192.svg',\\n cache.addAll(APP_SHELL))\\n  );\\n  // Activate immediately without waiting for tabs to close\\n  self.skipWaiting();\\n});\\n\\n/* ===== Activate: clean up old caches ===== */\\nself.addEventListener('activate', (event) => {\\n  event.waitUntil(\\n    caches.keys().then((keys) =>\\n      Promise.all(\\n        keys\\n          .filter((k) => k !== CACHE_NAME)\\n          .map((k) => caches.delete(k))\\n      )\\n    )\\n  );\\n  // Take control of all clients immediately\\n  self.clients.claim();\\n});\\n\\n/* ===== Fetch: cache-first for shell, network-first for API ===== */\\nself.addEventListener('fetch', (event) => {\\n  const { request } = event;\\n  const url = new URL(request.url);\\n\\n  // Only handle same-origin GET requests (don't intercept webhook POSTs)\\n  if (request.method !== 'GET' || url.origin !== self.location.origin) {\\n    return;\\n  }\\n\\n  event.respondWith(\\n    caches.match(request).then((cached) => {\\n      if (cached) return cached;\\n\\n      return fetch(request).then((response) => {\\n        // Cache successful responses for app shell resources\\n        if (response.ok) {\\n          const clone = response.clone();\\n          caches.open(CACHE_NAME).then((cache) => cache.put(request, clone));\\n        }\\n        return response;\\n      }).catch(() => {\\n        // Offline fallback \u2014 return index.html for navigation requests\\n        if (request.destination === 'document') {\\n          return caches.match('/index.html');\\n        }\\n      });\\n    })\\n  );\\n});\\n\"\n```"

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #55
Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the app. Let me set up Playwright test infrastructure. Here's what I know: 1. **App URL**: https://hey-chill.vercel.app 2. **HTML structure**: - Header: "Chill" heading, Settings button (`#settings-btn`) - Main messages area (`#messages`) with empty state (`#messages-empty`) - Transcript bar (`#transcript-bar`) - hidden by default, with Discard/Send buttons - Live transcript (`#live-transcript`) - hidden, shown while recording - Mic section: hint text (`#mic-hint`), mic button (`#mic-btn`) - Not supported banner (`#not-supported`) - Settings modal (`#modal-overlay`) with webhook URL input 3. **JavaScript logic**: - Web Speech API (`SpeechRecognition`) - `isListening` state - `finalTranscript`, `interimText` - `SILENCE_DELAY = 3500` ms - Settings saved to `localStorage.webhookUrl` - Webhook POST with `{ text, source: 'hey-chill', timestamp }` - Message status: 'sending', 'sent', 'error', 'no-webhook' 4. **Service Worker** handles the webhook requests and manages message persistence. 5. **Manifest** configures the app as a standalone PWA called "Hey Chill". Now I'm planning out the test suite with Playwright. I'll need configuration files and a tests directory covering UI rendering, the settings modal workflow, push-to-talk button interactions, transcription display, and the voice activity detection silence timer. For the MockSpeechRecognition, I need to inject a mock into the test environment that exposes the standard Web Speech API interface—`window.SpeechRecognition` with event handlers like `onstart`, `onresult`, and `onend`. The mock needs to respond to `start()` and `stop()` calls and fire result events with the expected structure containing transcript data. I need to inject a mock SpeechRecognition before the page loads, and I can do this by defining it in `page.addInitScript()` so it's available when app.js runs. The mock should keep track of instances so the test can interact with them, and I'll use `page.exposeFunction()` to bridge communication between the test code and the page context. Setting up the mock class... I'm completing the mock speech recognition implementation by finishing the result event structure, then exposing it to the window so tests can trigger speech events. For VAD testing with fake timers, I'll use the clock utilities, and for webhook validation I'll intercept fetch calls through the routing system. Now I'm mapping out the full test suite structure across multiple spec files, starting with UI tests that verify the page loads correctly with the expected header and controls visible. - Mic button is visible and says "Start recording" - Mic hint says "Tap to speak" - Messages empty state is shown on first load - Transcript bar is hidden on load - Live transcript is hidden on load The settings modal tests cover opening and closing behavior through various interactions, along with webhook URL input and localStorage persistence. The push-to-talk tests verify the recording flow—mic state changes, UI updates during recording, and transcript visibility toggling. The transcription tests check that interim and final transcripts display correctly, HTML gets escaped, and the UI responds appropriately to empty or populated transcript states. The VAD tests are starting to cover voice activity detection... Auto-stops after 3.5 seconds of silence, resets the timer when speech resumes, shows the transcript after stopping, clears the timer on manual stop, and starts immediately when recording begins. The webhook tests verify that messages send to the correct endpoint with proper payload structure, handle various response scenarios like 404s and network errors, and show appropriate status messages. The PWA tests ensure the manifest is properly configured with required fields and icons, the service worker registers and caches the app shell, and the unsupported banner displays correctly. That's 54 tests total across all specs. For the test infrastructure, I need to set up Playwright, create a config file, and build shared fixtures for mocking speech recognition. Now I'm thinking through the VAD silence detection tests more carefully. The app uses `setTimeout` for the silence timer, so I'll need to use Playwright's clock manipulation to advance time and verify the auto-stop behavior. The key is installing the fake clock before the page evaluates any timer code, so I'll need to call `page.clock.install()` before navigating to ensure the timers get intercepted properly. I'm settling on a fixture-based approach where most tests use a basic setup that injects the mock speech recognition before navigation, and VAD tests specifically install the fake clock first to control timing around the silence detection logic. Now I'm ready to implement the MockSpeechRecognition class... Writing mock recognition class... Writing test cases... Actually, I need to check how the app handles the `onend` and `onresult` event flow in the speech recognition lifecycle to make sure my mock implementation matches the real behavior. Looking at the code, when `stopListening()` is called, it sets `isListening` to false before calling `recognition.stop()`, which prevents `finishRecording()` from being triggered twice—once from the `onend` handler and once from the stop call itself. The silence timer flow works by starting a 3500ms timer on recording start, and when it fires, `stopListening()` gets called, which sets the flag, stops recognition, and finishes the recording. Since `isListening` is already false by the time `onend` fires, the callback doesn't duplicate the finish logic. For the mock implementation, I need to simulate what happens when `stop()` is called. So I need to make sure the VAD test actually has a transcript to display—that means simulating a recognition result before the silence timer fires, which will reset the timer since `resetSilenceTimer()` gets called in `onresult`. The auto-stop should trigger 3500ms after that last result. I'm mapping out the test scenarios now—one for basic silence timeout where the timer fires after 3500ms with no speech, another for verifying the timer resets when a result comes in, and a third to confirm the mic button state updates correctly when recording stops. I've got the test structure clear, so I'm ready to start writing the code.

  "content": {
    "type": "thinking",
    "thinking": "Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the app. Let me set up Playwright test infrastructure.\n\nHere's what I know:\n1. **App URL**: https://hey-chill.vercel.app\n2. **HTML structure**: \n   - Header: \"Chill\" heading, Settings button (`#settings-btn`)\n   - Main messages area (`#messages`) with empty state (`#messages-empty`)\n   - Transcript bar (`#transcript-bar`) - hidden by default, with Discard/Send buttons\n   - Live transcript (`#live-transcript`) - hidden, shown while recording\n   - Mic section: hint text (`#mic-hint`), mic button (`#mic-btn`)\n   - Not supported banner (`#not-supported`)\n   - Settings modal (`#modal-overlay`) with webhook URL input\n\n3. **JavaScript logic**:\n   - Web Speech API (`SpeechRecognition`)\n   - `isListening` state\n   - `finalTranscript`, `interimText`\n   - `SILENCE_DELAY = 3500` ms\n   - Settings saved to `localStorage.webhookUrl`\n   - Webhook POST with `{ text, source: 'hey-chill', timestamp }`\n   - Message status: 'sending', 'sent', 'error', 'no-webhook'\n\n4. **Service Worker** handles the webhook requests and manages message persistence.\n\n5. **Manifest** configures the app as a standalone PWA called \"Hey Chill\".\n\nNow I'm planning out the test suite with Playwright. I'll need configuration files and a tests directory covering UI rendering, the settings modal workflow, push-to-talk button interactions, transcription display, and the voice activity detection silence timer.\n\nFor the MockSpeechRecognition, I need to inject a mock into the test environment that exposes the standard Web Speech API interface\u2014`window.SpeechRecognition` with event handlers like `onstart`, `onresult`, and `onend`. The mock needs to respond to `start()` and `stop()` calls and fire result events with the expected structure containing transcript data. I need to inject a mock SpeechRecognition before the page loads, and I can do this by defining it in `page.addInitScript()` so it's available when app.js runs. The mock should keep track of instances so the test can interact with them, and I'll use `page.exposeFunction()` to bridge communication between the test code and the page context.\n\nSetting up the mock class...\n\nI'm completing the mock speech recognition implementation by finishing the result event structure, then exposing it to the window so tests can trigger speech events. For VAD testing with fake timers, I'll use the clock utilities, and for webhook validation I'll intercept fetch calls through the routing system. Now I'm mapping out the full test suite structure across multiple spec files, starting with UI tests that verify the page loads correctly with the expected header and controls visible. - Mic button is visible and says \"Start recording\"\n- Mic hint says \"Tap to speak\"\n- Messages empty state is shown on first load\n- Transcript bar is hidden on load\n- Live transcript is hidden on load\n\nThe settings modal tests cover opening and closing behavior through various interactions, along with webhook URL input and localStorage persistence. The push-to-talk tests verify the recording flow\u2014mic state changes, UI updates during recording, and transcript visibility toggling. The transcription tests check that interim and final transcripts display correctly, HTML gets escaped, and the UI responds appropriately to empty or populated transcript states. The VAD tests are starting to cover voice activity detection...\n\nAuto-stops after 3.5 seconds of silence, resets the timer when speech resumes, shows the transcript after stopping, clears the timer on manual stop, and starts immediately when recording begins. The webhook tests verify that messages send to the correct endpoint with proper payload structure, handle various response scenarios like 404s and network errors, and show appropriate status messages. The PWA tests ensure the manifest is properly configured with required fields and icons, the service worker registers and caches the app shell, and the unsupported banner displays correctly. That's 54 tests total across all specs. For the test infrastructure, I need to set up Playwright, create a config file, and build shared fixtures for mocking speech recognition.\n\nNow I'm thinking through the VAD silence detection tests more carefully. The app uses `setTimeout` for the silence timer, so I'll need to use Playwright's clock manipulation to advance time and verify the auto-stop behavior. The key is installing the fake clock before the page evaluates any timer code, so I'll need to call `page.clock.install()` before navigating to ensure the timers get intercepted properly. I'm settling on a fixture-based approach where most tests use a basic setup that injects the mock speech recognition before navigation, and VAD tests specifically install the fake clock first to control timing around the silence detection logic. Now I'm ready to implement the MockSpeechRecognition class...\n\nWriting mock recognition class... Writing test cases...\n\nActually, I need to check how the app handles the `onend` and `onresult` event flow in the speech recognition lifecycle to make sure my mock implementation matches the real behavior.\n\nLooking at the code, when `stopListening()` is called, it sets `isListening` to false before calling `recognition.stop()`, which prevents `finishRecording()` from being triggered twice\u2014once from the `onend` handler and once from the stop call itself.\n\nThe silence timer flow works by starting a 3500ms timer on recording start, and when it fires, `stopListening()` gets called, which sets the flag, stops recognition, and finishes the recording. Since `isListening` is already false by the time `onend` fires, the callback doesn't duplicate the finish logic.\n\nFor the mock implementation, I need to simulate what happens when `stop()` is called. So I need to make sure the VAD test actually has a transcript to display\u2014that means simulating a recognition result before the silence timer fires, which will reset the timer since `resetSilenceTimer()` gets called in `onresult`. The auto-stop should trigger 3500ms after that last result. I'm mapping out the test scenarios now\u2014one for basic silence timeout where the timer fires after 3500ms with no speech, another for verifying the timer resets when a result comes in, and a third to confirm the mic button state updates correctly when recording stops. I've got the test structure clear, so I'm ready to start writing the code.",

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #71
Write TOOL CALL 
{
  "file_path": "/home/gem/project/tests/fixtures.ts",
  "content": "import { test as base, Page } from '@playwright/test';\n\nexport const APP_URL = 'https://hey-chill.vercel.app';\n\n/**\n * Script injected before page load to replace Web Speech API with a\n * controllable mock. Exposes window.__mockRecognition for test control.\n */\nexport const MOCK_SPEECH_SCRIPT = `\n(function() {\n  class MockSpeechRecognition {\n    constructor() {\n      this.continuous = false;\n      this.interimResults = false;\n      this.lang = '';\n      this.maxAlternatives = 1;\n      this.onstart = null;\n      this.onresult = null;\n      this.onerror = null;\n      this.onend = null;\n      this._started = false;\n      window.__mockRecognition = this;\n    }\n\n    start() {\n      this._started = true;\n      if (this.onstart) this.onstart();\n    }\n\n    stop() {\n      this._started = false;\n      if (this.onend) this.onend();\n    }\n\n    /** Simulate a speech result event */\n    _fireResult(transcript, isFinal) {\n      if (!this.onresult) return;\n      const results = [[{ transcript: transcript }]];\n      results[0].isFinal = !!isFinal;\n      results.length = 1;\n      this.onresult({ resultIndex: 0, results: results });\n    }\n\n    /** Simulate a recognition error */\n    _fireError(errorCode) {\n      if (this.onerror) this.onerror({ error: errorCode });\n    }\n\n    /** Simulate recognition ending on its own (e.g. network cut) */\n    _fireEnd() {\n      if (this.onend) this.onend();\n    }\n  }\n\n  window.SpeechRecognition = MockSpeechRecognition;\n  window.webkitSpeechRecognition = MockSpeechRecognition;\n})();\n`;\n\n/** Navigate to the app with MockSpeechRecognition pre-installed. */\nexport async function gotoWithMock(page: Page) {\n  await page.addInitScript(MOCK_SPEECH_SCRIPT);\n  await page.goto(APP_URL);\n}\n\n/** Simulate a final speech result via the mock. */\nexport async function simulateSpeech(page: Page, transcript: string) {\n  await page.evaluate(\n    (t) => (window as any).__mockRecognition._fireResult(t, true),\n    transcript,\n  );\n}\n\n/** Simulate an interim (non-final) speech result. */\nexport async function simulateInterim(page: Page, transcript: string) {\n  await page.evaluate(\n    (t) => (window as any).__mockRecognition._fireResult(t, false),\n    transcript,\n  );\n}\n\n/** Click mic to start recording, wait for aria-pressed=true. */\nexport async function startRecording(page: Page) {\n  await page.click('#mic-btn');\n  await page.waitForFunction(\n    () => document.getElementById('mic-btn')?.getAttribute('aria-pressed') === 'true',\n  );\n}\n\n/** Click mic to stop recording, wait for aria-pressed=false. */\nexport async function stopRecording(page: Page) {\n  await page.click('#mic-btn');\n  await page.waitForFunction(\n    () => document.getElementById('mic-btn')?.getAttribute('aria-pressed') === 'false',\n  );\n}\n\n// Custom fixture type\ntype Fixtures = {\n  mockPage: Page;\n};\n\n/** A page fixture that pre-installs the mock and navigates to the app. */\nexport const test = base.extend<Fixtures>({\n  mockPage: async ({ page }, use) => {\n    await gotoWithMock(page);\n    await use(page);\n  },\n});\n\nexport { expect } from '@playwright/test';\n"
}

      "content": "import { test as base, Page } from '@playwright/test';\n\nexport const APP_URL = 'https://hey-chill.vercel.app';\n\n/**\n * Script injected before page load to replace Web Speech API with a\n * controllable mock. Exposes window.__mockRecognition for test control.\n */\nexport const MOCK_SPEECH_SCRIPT = `\n(function() {\n  class MockSpeechRecognition {\n    constructor() {\n      this.continuous = false;\n      this.interimResults = false;\n      this.lang = '';\n      this.maxAlternatives = 1;\n      this.onstart = null;\n      this.onresult = null;\n      this.onerror = null;\n      this.onend = null;\n      this._started = false;\n      window.__mockRecognition = this;\n    }\n\n    start() {\n      this._started = true;\n      if (this.onstart) this.onstart();\n    }\n\n    stop() {\n      this._started = false;\n      if (this.onend) this.onend();\n    }\n\n    /** Simulate a speech result event */\n    _fireResult(transcript, isFinal) {\n      if (!this.onresult) return;\n      const results = [[{ transcript: transcript }]];\n      results[0].isFinal = !!isFinal;\n      results.length = 1;\n      this.onresult({ resultIndex: 0, results: results });\n    }\n\n    /** Simulate a recognition error */\n    _fireError(errorCode) {\n      if (this.onerror) this.onerror({ error: errorCode });\n    }\n\n    /** Simulate recognition ending on its own (e.g. network cut) */\n    _fireEnd() {\n      if (this.onend) this.onend();\n    }\n  }\n\n  window.SpeechRecognition = MockSpeechRecognition;\n  window.webkitSpeechRecognition = MockSpeechRecognition;\n})();\n`;\n\n/** Navigate to the app with MockSpeechRecognition pre-installed. */\nexport async function gotoWithMock(page: Page) {\n  await page.addInitScript(MOCK_SPEECH_SCRIPT);\n  await page.goto(APP_URL);\n}\n\n/** Simulate a final speech result via the mock. */\nexport async function simulateSpeech(page: Page, transcript: string) {\n  await page.evaluate(\n    (t) => (window as any).__mockRecognition._fireResult(t, true),\n    transcript,\n  );\n}\n\n/** Simulate an interim (non-final) speech result. */\nexport async function simulateInterim(page: Page, transcript: string) {\n  await page.evaluate(\n    (t) => (window as any).__mockRecognition._fireResult(t, false),\n    transcript,\n  );\n}\n\n/** Click mic to start recording, wait for aria-pressed=true. */\nexport async function startRecording(page: Page) {\n  await page.click('#mic-btn');\n  await page.waitForFunction(\n    () => document.getElementById('mic-btn')?.getAttribute('aria-pressed') === 'true',\n  );\n}\n\n/** Click mic to stop recording, wait for aria-pressed=false. */\nexport async function stopRecording(page: Page) {\n  await page.click('#mic-btn');\n  await page.waitForFunction(\n    () => document.getElementById('mic-btn')?.getAttribute('aria-pressed') === 'false',\n  );\n}\n\n// Custom fixture type\ntype Fixtures = {\n  mockPage: Page;\n};\n\n/** A page fixture that pre-installs the mock and navigates to the app. */\nexport const test = base.extend<Fixtures>({\n  mockPage: async ({ page }, use) => {\n    await gotoWithMock(page);\n    await use(page);\n  },\n});\n\nexport { expect } from '@playwright/test';\n"
    },
    "caller": {
      "type": "direct"
    }
  }
}

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #73
Write TOOL CALL 
{
  "file_path": "/home/gem/project/tests/ui.spec.ts",
  "content": "/**\n * UI Baseline Tests\n * Verifies page structure, initial state, and basic accessibility attributes.\n */\nimport { test, expect } from './fixtures';\n\ntest.describe('UI baseline', () => {\n  test('page has correct title', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    await expect(page).toHaveTitle('Hey Chill');\n  });\n\n  test('header displays \"Chill\" heading', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    await expect(page.locator('h1')).toHaveText('Chill');\n  });\n\n  test('settings button is visible', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    await expect(page.locator('#settings-btn')).toBeVisible();\n    await expect(page.locator('#settings-btn')).toHaveAttribute('aria-label', 'Settings');\n  });\n\n  test('mic button is visible with correct initial state', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    const btn = page.locator('#mic-btn');\n    await expect(btn).toBeVisible();\n    await expect(btn).toHaveAttribute('aria-label', 'Start recording');\n    await expect(btn).toHaveAttribute('aria-pressed', 'false');\n  });\n\n  test('mic hint shows \"Tap to speak\" initially', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    await expect(page.locator('#mic-hint')).toHaveText('Tap to speak');\n  });\n\n  test('messages empty state is visible on first load', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    await expect(page.locator('#messages-empty')).toBeVisible();\n    await expect(page.locator('#messages-empty p')).toContainText('Your messages will appear here');\n  });\n\n  test('transcript bar is hidden on load', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    await expect(page.locator('#transcript-bar')).toBeHidden();\n  });\n\n  test('live transcript is hidden on load', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    await expect(page.locator('#live-transcript')).toBeHidden();\n  });\n});\n"
}
▶ VIEW RAW DATA

  "content": {
    "type": "tool_use",
    "id": "toolu_01VJVfgZDTqH48sjZgkn6K8g",
    "name": "Write",
    "input": {
      "file_path": "/home/gem/project/tests/ui.spec.ts",
      "content": "/**\n * UI Baseline Tests\n * Verifies page structure, initial state, and basic accessibility attributes.\n */\nimport { test, expect } from './fixtures';\n\ntest.describe('UI baseline', () => {\n  test('page has correct title', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    await expect(page).toHaveTitle('Hey Chill');\n  });\n\n  test('header displays \"Chill\" heading', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    await expect(page.locator('h1')).toHaveText('Chill');\n  });\n\n  test('settings button is visible', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    await expect(page.locator('#settings-btn')).toBeVisible();\n    await expect(page.locator('#settings-btn')).toHaveAttribute('aria-label', 'Settings');\n  });\n\n  test('mic button is visible with correct initial state', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    const btn = page.locator('#mic-btn');\n    await expect(btn).toBeVisible();\n    await expect(btn).toHaveAttribute('aria-label', 'Start recording');\n    await expect(btn).toHaveAttribute('aria-pressed', 'false');\n  });\n\n  test('mic hint shows \"Tap to speak\" initially', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    await expect(page.locator('#mic-hint')).toHaveText('Tap to speak');\n  });\n\n  test('messages empty state is visible on first load', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    await expect(page.locator('#messages-empty')).toBeVisible();\n    await expect(page.locator('#messages-empty p')).toContainText('Your messages will appear here');\n  });\n\n  test('transcript bar is hidden on load', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    await expect(page.locator('#transcript-bar')).toBeHidden();\n  });\n\n  test('live transcript is hidden on load', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    await expect(page.locator('#live-transcript')).toBeHidden();\n  });\n});\n"
    },
    "caller": {
      "type": "direct"
    }
  }
}

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #75
Write TOOL CALL 
{
  "file_path": "/home/gem/project/tests/settings.spec.ts",
  "content": "/**\n * Settings Modal Tests\n * Covers opening/closing the settings panel and webhook URL persistence.\n */\nimport { test, expect } from './fixtures';\n\ntest.describe('Settings modal', () => {\n  test('opens when settings button is clicked', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    await page.click('#settings-btn');\n    await expect(page.locator('#modal-overlay')).not.toHaveAttribute('hidden');\n    await expect(page.locator('h2#modal-title')).toHaveText('Settings');\n  });\n\n  test('closes when X button is clicked', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    await page.click('#settings-btn');\n    await expect(page.locator('#modal-overlay')).not.toHaveAttribute('hidden');\n    await page.click('#modal-close');\n    await expect(page.locator('#modal-overlay')).toHaveAttribute('hidden', '');\n  });\n\n  test('closes when Escape key is pressed', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    await page.click('#settings-btn');\n    await expect(page.locator('#modal-overlay')).not.toHaveAttribute('hidden');\n    await page.keyboard.press('Escape');\n    await expect(page.locator('#modal-overlay')).toHaveAttribute('hidden', '');\n  });\n\n  test('closes when clicking outside the modal', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    await page.click('#settings-btn');\n    await expect(page.locator('#modal-overlay')).not.toHaveAttribute('hidden');\n    // Click on the overlay backdrop (not the inner modal box)\n    await page.locator('#modal-overlay').click({ position: { x: 5, y: 5 } });\n    await expect(page.locator('#modal-overlay')).toHaveAttribute('hidden', '');\n  });\n\n  test('webhook URL field accepts input', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    await page.click('#settings-btn');\n    await page.fill('#webhook-url', 'https://example.com/hook');\n    await expect(page.locator('#webhook-url')).toHaveValue('https://example.com/hook');\n  });\n\n  test('saving webhook URL persists to localStorage', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    await page.click('#settings-btn');\n    await page.fill('#webhook-url', 'https://example.com/hook');\n    await page.click('#save-settings');\n\n    const stored = await page.evaluate(() => localStorage.getItem('webhookUrl'));\n    expect(stored).toBe('https://example.com/hook');\n  });\n\n  test('saved URL is pre-populated when settings reopened', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    // Set via localStorage directly\n    await page.evaluate(() => localStorage.setItem('webhookUrl', 'https://saved.example.com/hook'));\n    await page.click('#settings-btn');\n    await expect(page.locator('#webhook-url')).toHaveValue('https://saved.example.com/hook');\n  });\n\n  test('Save button closes the modal', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    await page.click('#settings-btn');\n    await page.fill('#webhook-url', 'https://example.com/hook');\n    await page.click('#save-settings');\n    await expect(page.locator('#modal-overlay')).toHaveAttribute('hidden', '');\n  });\n});\n"
}

      "content": "/**\n * Settings Modal Tests\n * Covers opening/closing the settings panel and webhook URL persistence.\n */\nimport { test, expect } from './fixtures';\n\ntest.describe('Settings modal', () => {\n  test('opens when settings button is clicked', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    await page.click('#settings-btn');\n    await expect(page.locator('#modal-overlay')).not.toHaveAttribute('hidden');\n    await expect(page.locator('h2#modal-title')).toHaveText('Settings');\n  });\n\n  test('closes when X button is clicked', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    await page.click('#settings-btn');\n    await expect(page.locator('#modal-overlay')).not.toHaveAttribute('hidden');\n    await page.click('#modal-close');\n    await expect(page.locator('#modal-overlay')).toHaveAttribute('hidden', '');\n  });\n\n  test('closes when Escape key is pressed', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    await page.click('#settings-btn');\n    await expect(page.locator('#modal-overlay')).not.toHaveAttribute('hidden');\n    await page.keyboard.press('Escape');\n    await expect(page.locator('#modal-overlay')).toHaveAttribute('hidden', '');\n  });\n\n  test('closes when clicking outside the modal', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    await page.click('#settings-btn');\n    await expect(page.locator('#modal-overlay')).not.toHaveAttribute('hidden');\n    // Click on the overlay backdrop (not the inner modal box)\n    await page.locator('#modal-overlay').click({ position: { x: 5, y: 5 } });\n    await expect(page.locator('#modal-overlay')).toHaveAttribute('hidden', '');\n  });\n\n  test('webhook URL field accepts input', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    await page.click('#settings-btn');\n    await page.fill('#webhook-url', 'https://example.com/hook');\n    await expect(page.locator('#webhook-url')).toHaveValue('https://example.com/hook');\n  });\n\n  test('saving webhook URL persists to localStorage', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    await page.click('#settings-btn');\n    await page.fill('#webhook-url', 'https://example.com/hook');\n    await page.click('#save-settings');\n\n    const stored = await page.evaluate(() => localStorage.getItem('webhookUrl'));\n    expect(stored).toBe('https://example.com/hook');\n  });\n\n  test('saved URL is pre-populated when settings reopened', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    // Set via localStorage directly\n    await page.evaluate(() => localStorage.setItem('webhookUrl', 'https://saved.example.com/hook'));\n    await page.click('#settings-btn');\n    await expect(page.locator('#webhook-url')).toHaveValue('https://saved.example.com/hook');\n  });\n\n  test('Save button closes the modal', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    await page.click('#settings-btn');\n    await page.fill('#webhook-url', 'https://example.com/hook');\n    await page.click('#save-settings');\n    await expect(page.locator('#modal-overlay')).toHaveAttribute('hidden', '');\n  });\n});\n"
    },
    "caller": {
      "type": "direct"
    }
  }
}

{
  "file_path": "/home/gem/project/tests/push-to-talk.spec.ts",
  "content": "/**\n * Push-to-Talk Tests\n * Covers mic button toggle behaviour and recording state UI changes.\n */\nimport { test, expect, startRecording, stopRecording, simulateSpeech } from './fixtures';\n\ntest.describe('Push-to-talk', () => {\n  test('clicking mic transitions to recording state', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    await startRecording(page);\n    await expect(page.locator('#mic-btn')).toHaveAttribute('aria-pressed', 'true');\n  });\n\n  test('recording state: mic hint changes to \"Tap to stop\"', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    await startRecording(page);\n    await expect(page.locator('#mic-hint')).toHaveText('Tap to stop');\n  });\n\n  test('recording state: aria-label becomes \"Stop recording\"', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    await startRecording(page);\n    await expect(page.locator('#mic-btn')).toHaveAttribute('aria-label', 'Stop recording');\n  });\n\n  test('recording state: live transcript panel becomes visible', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    await startRecording(page);\n    await expect(page.locator('#live-transcript')).toBeVisible();\n  });\n\n  test('clicking mic again stops recording', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    await startRecording(page);\n    await stopRecording(page);\n    await expect(page.locator('#mic-btn')).toHaveAttribute('aria-pressed', 'false');\n  });\n\n  test('after stopping: live transcript is hidden', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    await startRecording(page);\n    await stopRecording(page);\n    await expect(page.locator('#live-transcript')).toBeHidden();\n  });\n\n  test('after stopping with transcript: transcript bar is shown', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    await startRecording(page);\n    await simulateSpeech(page, 'hello world');\n    await stopRecording(page);\n    await expect(page.locator('#transcript-bar')).toBeVisible();\n    await expect(page.locator('#transcript-text')).toHaveText('hello world');\n  });\n\n  test('after stopping without speech: transcript bar stays hidden', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    await startRecording(page);\n    // No speech simulated\n    await stopRecording(page);\n    await expect(page.locator('#transcript-bar')).toBeHidden();\n  });\n});\n"
}

{
  "file_path": "/home/gem/project/tests/transcription.spec.ts",
  "content": "/**\n * Transcription Display Tests\n * Covers live transcript updates, interim vs final results, and HTML escaping.\n */\nimport { test, expect, startRecording, stopRecording, simulateSpeech, simulateInterim } from './fixtures';\n\ntest.describe('Transcription', () => {\n  test('live text starts as \"Listening\u2026\" when recording begins', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    await startRecording(page);\n    await expect(page.locator('#live-text')).toHaveText('Listening\u2026');\n  });\n\n  test('interim transcript updates live text', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    await startRecording(page);\n    await simulateInterim(page, 'hello');\n    await expect(page.locator('#live-text')).toHaveText('hello');\n  });\n\n  test('final transcript updates live text', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    await startRecording(page);\n    await simulateSpeech(page, 'hello world');\n    await expect(page.locator('#live-text')).toHaveText('hello world');\n  });\n\n  test('multiple final results are concatenated', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    await startRecording(page);\n    await simulateSpeech(page, 'hello');\n    await simulateSpeech(page, 'world');\n    // The app appends with a space: 'hello ' + 'world '\n    await expect(page.locator('#live-text')).toContainText('hello');\n    await expect(page.locator('#live-text')).toContainText('world');\n  });\n\n  test('transcript bar shows correct text after recording stops', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    await startRecording(page);\n    await simulateSpeech(page, 'test message');\n    await stopRecording(page);\n    await expect(page.locator('#transcript-text')).toHaveText('test message');\n  });\n\n  test('HTML special characters are escaped in transcript display', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    await startRecording(page);\n    await simulateSpeech(page, '<script>alert(1)</script>');\n    await stopRecording(page);\n    // The text content should be the raw string, not executed HTML\n    await expect(page.locator('#transcript-text')).toHaveText('<script>alert(1)</script>');\n    // But the DOM should not contain a script element from user input\n    const scriptCount = await page.locator('#messages script').count();\n    expect(scriptCount).toBe(0);\n  });\n\n  test('discard button hides the transcript bar', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    await startRecording(page);\n    await simulateSpeech(page, 'discard me');\n    await stopRecording(page);\n    await expect(page.locator('#transcript-bar')).toBeVisible();\n    await page.click('#discard-btn');\n    await expect(page.locator('#transcript-bar')).toBeHidden();\n  });\n\n  test('live transcript panel is hidden after stopping', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    await startRecording(page);\n    await simulateSpeech(page, 'something');\n    await stopRecording(page);\n    await expect(page.locator('#live-transcript')).toBeHidden();\n  });\n});\n"
}

      "content": "/**\n * Transcription Display Tests\n * Covers live transcript updates, interim vs final results, and HTML escaping.\n */\nimport { test, expect, startRecording, stopRecording, simulateSpeech, simulateInterim } from './fixtures';\n\ntest.describe('Transcription', () => {\n  test('live text starts as \"Listening\u2026\" when recording begins', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    await startRecording(page);\n    await expect(page.locator('#live-text')).toHaveText('Listening\u2026');\n  });\n\n  test('interim transcript updates live text', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    await startRecording(page);\n    await simulateInterim(page, 'hello');\n    await expect(page.locator('#live-text')).toHaveText('hello');\n  });\n\n  test('final transcript updates live text', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    await startRecording(page);\n    await simulateSpeech(page, 'hello world');\n    await expect(page.locator('#live-text')).toHaveText('hello world');\n  });\n\n  test('multiple final results are concatenated', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    await startRecording(page);\n    await simulateSpeech(page, 'hello');\n    await simulateSpeech(page, 'world');\n    // The app appends with a space: 'hello ' + 'world '\n    await expect(page.locator('#live-text')).toContainText('hello');\n    await expect(page.locator('#live-text')).toContainText('world');\n  });\n\n  test('transcript bar shows correct text after recording stops', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    await startRecording(page);\n    await simulateSpeech(page, 'test message');\n    await stopRecording(page);\n    await expect(page.locator('#transcript-text')).toHaveText('test message');\n  });\n\n  test('HTML special characters are escaped in transcript display', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    await startRecording(page);\n    await simulateSpeech(page, '<script>alert(1)</script>');\n    await stopRecording(page);\n    // The text content should be the raw string, not executed HTML\n    await expect(page.locator('#transcript-text')).toHaveText('<script>alert(1)</script>');\n    // But the DOM should not contain a script element from user input\n    const scriptCount = await page.locator('#messages script').count();\n    expect(scriptCount).toBe(0);\n  });\n\n  test('discard button hides the transcript bar', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    await startRecording(page);\n    await simulateSpeech(page, 'discard me');\n    await stopRecording(page);\n    await expect(page.locator('#transcript-bar')).toBeVisible();\n    await page.click('#discard-btn');\n    await expect(page.locator('#transcript-bar')).toBeHidden();\n  });\n\n  test('live transcript panel is hidden after stopping', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    await startRecording(page);\n    await simulateSpeech(page, 'something');\n    await stopRecording(page);\n    await expect(page.locator('#live-transcript')).toBeHidden();\n  });\n});\n"

    }
  }

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #81
Write TOOL CALL 
{
  "file_path": "/home/gem/project/tests/vad.spec.ts",
  "content": "/**\n * Voice Activity Detection (VAD) Tests\n * Tests the 3500 ms silence auto-stop using page.clock fake timers.\n *\n * Strategy: page.clock.install() before navigation so that setTimeout calls\n * in app.js use fake time. The MockSpeechRecognition is also injected via\n * addInitScript before load.\n */\nimport { test as base, expect } from '@playwright/test';\nimport { APP_URL, MOCK_SPEECH_SCRIPT, startRecording } from './fixtures';\n\n// VAD tests need clock installed before navigation \u2014 use a custom setup\nconst test = base;\n\ntest.describe('VAD silence auto-stop', () => {\n  test('auto-stops recording after 3500 ms of silence', async ({ page }) => {\n    await page.clock.install();\n    await page.addInitScript(MOCK_SPEECH_SCRIPT);\n    await page.goto(APP_URL);\n\n    await startRecording(page);\n    await expect(page.locator('#mic-btn')).toHaveAttribute('aria-pressed', 'true');\n\n    await page.clock.tick(3500);\n\n    await expect(page.locator('#mic-btn')).toHaveAttribute('aria-pressed', 'false');\n  });\n\n  test('silence timer resets when speech arrives, delaying auto-stop', async ({ page }) => {\n    await page.clock.install();\n    await page.addInitScript(MOCK_SPEECH_SCRIPT);\n    await page.goto(APP_URL);\n\n    await startRecording(page);\n\n    // Advance 2000 ms \u2014 still within the first 3500 ms window\n    await page.clock.tick(2000);\n    await expect(page.locator('#mic-btn')).toHaveAttribute('aria-pressed', 'true');\n\n    // Speech arrives \u2014 resets the 3500 ms timer\n    await page.evaluate(() => (window as any).__mockRecognition._fireResult('hello', true));\n\n    // Advance another 2000 ms (total 4000 ms, but only 2000 since last speech)\n    await page.clock.tick(2000);\n    // Should still be recording\n    await expect(page.locator('#mic-btn')).toHaveAttribute('aria-pressed', 'true');\n\n    // Advance the remaining 1500 ms \u2014 total 3500 ms after last speech\n    await page.clock.tick(1500);\n    await expect(page.locator('#mic-btn')).toHaveAttribute('aria-pressed', 'false');\n  });\n\n  test('after VAD auto-stop, transcript bar shows the captured text', async ({ page }) => {\n    await page.clock.install();\n    await page.addInitScript(MOCK_SPEECH_SCRIPT);\n    await page.goto(APP_URL);\n\n    await startRecording(page);\n    await page.evaluate(() => (window as any).__mockRecognition._fireResult('voice activity test', true));\n\n    await page.clock.tick(3500);\n\n    await expect(page.locator('#transcript-bar')).toBeVisible();\n    await expect(page.locator('#transcript-text')).toContainText('voice activity test');\n  });\n\n  test('manual stop clears the silence timer (no double-stop)', async ({ page }) => {\n    await page.clock.install();\n    await page.addInitScript(MOCK_SPEECH_SCRIPT);\n    await page.goto(APP_URL);\n\n    await startRecording(page);\n    await page.evaluate(() => (window as any).__mockRecognition._fireResult('manual stop', true));\n\n    // Stop manually before timer fires\n    await page.click('#mic-btn');\n    await expect(page.locator('#mic-btn')).toHaveAttribute('aria-pressed', 'false');\n\n    // Advance past 3500 ms \u2014 should not cause errors or state flipping\n    await page.clock.tick(5000);\n    await expect(page.locator('#mic-btn')).toHaveAttribute('aria-pressed', 'false');\n    await expect(page.locator('#transcript-bar')).toBeVisible();\n  });\n\n  test('silence timer starts immediately when recording begins', async ({ page }) => {\n    await page.clock.install();\n    await page.addInitScript(MOCK_SPEECH_SCRIPT);\n    await page.goto(APP_URL);\n\n    await startRecording(page);\n\n    // Should still be recording at 3499 ms\n    await page.clock.tick(3499);\n    await expect(page.locator('#mic-btn')).toHaveAttribute('aria-pressed', 'true');\n\n    // Auto-stops at exactly 3500 ms\n    await page.clock.tick(1);\n    await expect(page.locator('#mic-btn')).toHaveAttribute('aria-pressed', 'false');\n  });\n});\n"
}
▶ VIEW RAW DATA

    "input": {
      "file_path": "/home/gem/project/tests/vad.spec.ts",
      "content": "/**\n * Voice Activity Detection (VAD) Tests\n * Tests the 3500 ms silence auto-stop using page.clock fake timers.\n *\n * Strategy: page.clock.install() before navigation so that setTimeout calls\n * in app.js use fake time. The MockSpeechRecognition is also injected via\n * addInitScript before load.\n */\nimport { test as base, expect } from '@playwright/test';\nimport { APP_URL, MOCK_SPEECH_SCRIPT, startRecording } from './fixtures';\n\n// VAD tests need clock installed before navigation \u2014 use a custom setup\nconst test = base;\n\ntest.describe('VAD silence auto-stop', () => {\n  test('auto-stops recording after 3500 ms of silence', async ({ page }) => {\n    await page.clock.install();\n    await page.addInitScript(MOCK_SPEECH_SCRIPT);\n    await page.goto(APP_URL);\n\n    await startRecording(page);\n    await expect(page.locator('#mic-btn')).toHaveAttribute('aria-pressed', 'true');\n\n    await page.clock.tick(3500);\n\n    await expect(page.locator('#mic-btn')).toHaveAttribute('aria-pressed', 'false');\n  });\n\n  test('silence timer resets when speech arrives, delaying auto-stop', async ({ page }) => {\n    await page.clock.install();\n    await page.addInitScript(MOCK_SPEECH_SCRIPT);\n    await page.goto(APP_URL);\n\n    await startRecording(page);\n\n    // Advance 2000 ms \u2014 still within the first 3500 ms window\n    await page.clock.tick(2000);\n    await expect(page.locator('#mic-btn')).toHaveAttribute('aria-pressed', 'true');\n\n    // Speech arrives \u2014 resets the 3500 ms timer\n    await page.evaluate(() => (window as any).__mockRecognition._fireResult('hello', true));\n\n    // Advance another 2000 ms (total 4000 ms, but only 2000 since last speech)\n    await page.clock.tick(2000);\n    // Should still be recording\n    await expect(page.locator('#mic-btn')).toHaveAttribute('aria-pressed', 'true');\n\n    // Advance the remaining 1500 ms \u2014 total 3500 ms after last speech\n    await page.clock.tick(1500);\n    await expect(page.locator('#mic-btn')).toHaveAttribute('aria-pressed', 'false');\n  });\n\n  test('after VAD auto-stop, transcript bar shows the captured text', async ({ page }) => {\n    await page.clock.install();\n    await page.addInitScript(MOCK_SPEECH_SCRIPT);\n    await page.goto(APP_URL);\n\n    await startRecording(page);\n    await page.evaluate(() => (window as any).__mockRecognition._fireResult('voice activity test', true));\n\n    await page.clock.tick(3500);\n\n    await expect(page.locator('#transcript-bar')).toBeVisible();\n    await expect(page.locator('#transcript-text')).toContainText('voice activity test');\n  });\n\n  test('manual stop clears the silence timer (no double-stop)', async ({ page }) => {\n    await page.clock.install();\n    await page.addInitScript(MOCK_SPEECH_SCRIPT);\n    await page.goto(APP_URL);\n\n    await startRecording(page);\n    await page.evaluate(() => (window as any).__mockRecognition._fireResult('manual stop', true));\n\n    // Stop manually before timer fires\n    await page.click('#mic-btn');\n    await expect(page.locator('#mic-btn')).toHaveAttribute('aria-pressed', 'false');\n\n    // Advance past 3500 ms \u2014 should not cause errors or state flipping\n    await page.clock.tick(5000);\n    await expect(page.locator('#mic-btn')).toHaveAttribute('aria-pressed', 'false');\n    await expect(page.locator('#transcript-bar')).toBeVisible();\n  });\n\n  test('silence timer starts immediately when recording begins', async ({ page }) => {\n    await page.clock.install();\n    await page.addInitScript(MOCK_SPEECH_SCRIPT);\n    await page.goto(APP_URL);\n\n    await startRecording(page);\n\n    // Should still be recording at 3499 ms\n    await page.clock.tick(3499);\n    await expect(page.locator('#mic-btn')).toHaveAttribute('aria-pressed', 'true');\n\n    // Auto-stops at exactly 3500 ms\n    await page.clock.tick(1);\n    await expect(page.locator('#mic-btn')).toHaveAttribute('aria-pressed', 'false');\n  });\n});\n"
    },
    "caller": {
      "type": "direct"
    }
  }
}

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #83
Write TOOL CALL 
{
  "file_path": "/home/gem/project/tests/webhook.spec.ts",
  "content": "/**\n * Webhook Send Tests\n * Uses page.route() to intercept fetch calls \u2014 no real webhook needed.\n */\nimport { test, expect, startRecording, stopRecording, simulateSpeech } from './fixtures';\n\nconst WEBHOOK = 'https://example.com/webhook/hey-chill';\n\ntest.describe('Webhook send', () => {\n  test('Send button POSTs transcript to the configured webhook URL', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    // Intercept the webhook call\n    let capturedRequest: { url: string; method: string; body: any } | null = null;\n    await page.route(WEBHOOK, async (route) => {\n      const req = route.request();\n      capturedRequest = {\n        url: req.url(),\n        method: req.method(),\n        body: JSON.parse(req.postData() || '{}'),\n      };\n      await route.fulfill({ status: 200, body: 'ok' });\n    });\n\n    await page.evaluate((url) => localStorage.setItem('webhookUrl', url), WEBHOOK);\n    await page.reload();\n\n    await startRecording(page);\n    await simulateSpeech(page, 'send this message');\n    await stopRecording(page);\n\n    await page.click('#send-btn');\n\n    await expect.poll(() => capturedRequest).not.toBeNull();\n    expect(capturedRequest!.method).toBe('POST');\n    expect(capturedRequest!.body.text).toBe('send this message');\n  });\n\n  test('webhook payload contains source field set to \"hey-chill\"', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    let body: any = null;\n    await page.route(WEBHOOK, async (route) => {\n      body = JSON.parse(route.request().postData() || '{}');\n      await route.fulfill({ status: 200, body: 'ok' });\n    });\n\n    await page.evaluate((url) => localStorage.setItem('webhookUrl', url), WEBHOOK);\n    await page.reload();\n\n    await startRecording(page);\n    await simulateSpeech(page, 'source test');\n    await stopRecording(page);\n    await page.click('#send-btn');\n\n    await expect.poll(() => body).not.toBeNull();\n    expect(body.source).toBe('hey-chill');\n  });\n\n  test('webhook payload contains a timestamp field', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    let body: any = null;\n    await page.route(WEBHOOK, async (route) => {\n      body = JSON.parse(route.request().postData() || '{}');\n      await route.fulfill({ status: 200, body: 'ok' });\n    });\n\n    await page.evaluate((url) => localStorage.setItem('webhookUrl', url), WEBHOOK);\n    await page.reload();\n\n    await startRecording(page);\n    await simulateSpeech(page, 'timestamp test');\n    await stopRecording(page);\n    await page.click('#send-btn');\n\n    await expect.poll(() => body).not.toBeNull();\n    expect(body.timestamp).toBeTruthy();\n    expect(() => new Date(body.timestamp)).not.toThrow();\n  });\n\n  test('message appears in history with \"Sent\" status on 200 response', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    await page.route(WEBHOOK, (route) => route.fulfill({ status: 200, body: 'ok' }));\n    await page.evaluate((url) => localStorage.setItem('webhookUrl', url), WEBHOOK);\n    await page.reload();\n\n    await startRecording(page);\n    await simulateSpeech(page, 'sent message');\n    await stopRecording(page);\n    await page.click('#send-btn');\n\n    const msg = page.locator('.message').first();\n    await expect(msg).toBeVisible();\n    await expect(msg.locator('.message-text')).toHaveText('sent message');\n    await expect(msg.locator('.message-status')).toHaveText('Sent');\n  });\n\n  test('message shows \"Failed\" status on non-2xx response', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    await page.route(WEBHOOK, (route) => route.fulfill({ status: 500, body: 'error' }));\n    await page.evaluate((url) => localStorage.setItem('webhookUrl', url), WEBHOOK);\n    await page.reload();\n\n    await startRecording(page);\n    await simulateSpeech(page, 'failing message');\n    await stopRecording(page);\n    await page.click('#send-btn');\n\n    const msg = page.locator('.message').first();\n    await expect(msg.locator('.message-status')).toHaveText('Failed');\n  });\n\n  test('message shows \"Failed\" status on network error', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    await page.route(WEBHOOK, (route) => route.abort('failed'));\n    await page.evaluate((url) => localStorage.setItem('webhookUrl', url), WEBHOOK);\n    await page.reload();\n\n    await startRecording(page);\n    await simulateSpeech(page, 'network error');\n    await stopRecording(page);\n    await page.click('#send-btn');\n\n    const msg = page.locator('.message').first();\n    await expect(msg.locator('.message-status')).toHaveText('Failed');\n  });\n\n  test('message shows \"No webhook set\" when no webhook URL is configured', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    // Ensure no webhook URL in localStorage\n    await page.evaluate(() => localStorage.removeItem('webhookUrl'));\n    await page.reload();\n\n    await startRecording(page);\n    await simulateSpeech(page, 'no webhook');\n    await stopRecording(page);\n    await page.click('#send-btn');\n\n    const msg = page.locator('.message').first();\n    await expect(msg.locator('.message-status')).toHaveText('No webhook set');\n  });\n\n  test('Discard button discards transcript without making a network request', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    let requestMade = false;\n    await page.route('**/*', async (route) => {\n      if (route.request().method() === 'POST') requestMade = true;\n      await route.continue();\n    });\n\n    await startRecording(page);\n    await simulateSpeech(page, 'do not send');\n    await stopRecording(page);\n    await page.click('#discard-btn');\n\n    // No message in history\n    await expect(page.locator('.message')).toHaveCount(0);\n    expect(requestMade).toBe(false);\n  });\n\n  test('sent message text is HTML-escaped in the DOM', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    await page.route(WEBHOOK, (route) => route.fulfill({ status: 200, body: 'ok' }));\n    await page.evaluate((url) => localStorage.setItem('webhookUrl', url), WEBHOOK);\n    await page.reload();\n\n    await startRecording(page);\n    await simulateSpeech(page, '<b>bold</b>');\n    await stopRecording(page);\n    await page.click('#send-btn');\n\n    const msg = page.locator('.message').first();\n    // Text content should be the literal string, not rendered HTML\n    await expect(msg.locator('.message-text')).toHaveText('<b>bold</b>');\n    // No <b> element should be injected inside message text\n    await expect(msg.locator('.message-text b')).toHaveCount(0);\n  });\n\n  test('empty messages area is hidden once a message is added', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    await page.route(WEBHOOK, (route) => route.fulfill({ status: 200, body: 'ok' }));\n    await page.evaluate((url) => localStorage.setItem('webhookUrl', url), WEBHOOK);\n    await page.reload();\n\n    await expect(page.locator('#messages-empty')).toBeVisible();\n\n    await startRecording(page);\n    await simulateSpeech(page, 'hide empty state');\n    await stopRecording(page);\n    await page.click('#send-btn');\n\n    await expect(page.locator('#messages-empty')).toBeHidden();\n  });\n});\n"
}

      "content": "/**\n * Webhook Send Tests\n * Uses page.route() to intercept fetch calls \u2014 no real webhook needed.\n */\nimport { test, expect, startRecording, stopRecording, simulateSpeech } from './fixtures';\n\nconst WEBHOOK = 'https://example.com/webhook/hey-chill';\n\ntest.describe('Webhook send', () => {\n  test('Send button POSTs transcript to the configured webhook URL', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    // Intercept the webhook call\n    let capturedRequest: { url: string; method: string; body: any } | null = null;\n    await page.route(WEBHOOK, async (route) => {\n      const req = route.request();\n      capturedRequest = {\n        url: req.url(),\n        method: req.method(),\n        body: JSON.parse(req.postData() || '{}'),\n      };\n      await route.fulfill({ status: 200, body: 'ok' });\n    });\n\n    await page.evaluate((url) => localStorage.setItem('webhookUrl', url), WEBHOOK);\n    await page.reload();\n\n    await startRecording(page);\n    await simulateSpeech(page, 'send this message');\n    await stopRecording(page);\n\n    await page.click('#send-btn');\n\n    await expect.poll(() => capturedRequest).not.toBeNull();\n    expect(capturedRequest!.method).toBe('POST');\n    expect(capturedRequest!.body.text).toBe('send this message');\n  });\n\n  test('webhook payload contains source field set to \"hey-chill\"', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    let body: any = null;\n    await page.route(WEBHOOK, async (route) => {\n      body = JSON.parse(route.request().postData() || '{}');\n      await route.fulfill({ status: 200, body: 'ok' });\n    });\n\n    await page.evaluate((url) => localStorage.setItem('webhookUrl', url), WEBHOOK);\n    await page.reload();\n\n    await startRecording(page);\n    await simulateSpeech(page, 'source test');\n    await stopRecording(page);\n    await page.click('#send-btn');\n\n    await expect.poll(() => body).not.toBeNull();\n    expect(body.source).toBe('hey-chill');\n  });\n\n  test('webhook payload contains a timestamp field', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    let body: any = null;\n    await page.route(WEBHOOK, async (route) => {\n      body = JSON.parse(route.request().postData() || '{}');\n      await route.fulfill({ status: 200, body: 'ok' });\n    });\n\n    await page.evaluate((url) => localStorage.setItem('webhookUrl', url), WEBHOOK);\n    await page.reload();\n\n    await startRecording(page);\n    await simulateSpeech(page, 'timestamp test');\n    await stopRecording(page);\n    await page.click('#send-btn');\n\n    await expect.poll(() => body).not.toBeNull();\n    expect(body.timestamp).toBeTruthy();\n    expect(() => new Date(body.timestamp)).not.toThrow();\n  });\n\n  test('message appears in history with \"Sent\" status on 200 response', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    await page.route(WEBHOOK, (route) => route.fulfill({ status: 200, body: 'ok' }));\n    await page.evaluate((url) => localStorage.setItem('webhookUrl', url), WEBHOOK);\n    await page.reload();\n\n    await startRecording(page);\n    await simulateSpeech(page, 'sent message');\n    await stopRecording(page);\n    await page.click('#send-btn');\n\n    const msg = page.locator('.message').first();\n    await expect(msg).toBeVisible();\n    await expect(msg.locator('.message-text')).toHaveText('sent message');\n    await expect(msg.locator('.message-status')).toHaveText('Sent');\n  });\n\n  test('message shows \"Failed\" status on non-2xx response', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    await page.route(WEBHOOK, (route) => route.fulfill({ status: 500, body: 'error' }));\n    await page.evaluate((url) => localStorage.setItem('webhookUrl', url), WEBHOOK);\n    await page.reload();\n\n    await startRecording(page);\n    await simulateSpeech(page, 'failing message');\n    await stopRecording(page);\n    await page.click('#send-btn');\n\n    const msg = page.locator('.message').first();\n    await expect(msg.locator('.message-status')).toHaveText('Failed');\n  });\n\n  test('message shows \"Failed\" status on network error', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    await page.route(WEBHOOK, (route) => route.abort('failed'));\n    await page.evaluate((url) => localStorage.setItem('webhookUrl', url), WEBHOOK);\n    await page.reload();\n\n    await startRecording(page);\n    await simulateSpeech(page, 'network error');\n    await stopRecording(page);\n    await page.click('#send-btn');\n\n    const msg = page.locator('.message').first();\n    await expect(msg.locator('.message-status')).toHaveText('Failed');\n  });\n\n  test('message shows \"No webhook set\" when no webhook URL is configured', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    // Ensure no webhook URL in localStorage\n    await page.evaluate(() => localStorage.removeItem('webhookUrl'));\n    await page.reload();\n\n    await startRecording(page);\n    await simulateSpeech(page, 'no webhook');\n    await stopRecording(page);\n    await page.click('#send-btn');\n\n    const msg = page.locator('.message').first();\n    await expect(msg.locator('.message-status')).toHaveText('No webhook set');\n  });\n\n  test('Discard button discards transcript without making a network request', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    let requestMade = false;\n    await page.route('**/*', async (route) => {\n      if (route.request().method() === 'POST') requestMade = true;\n      await route.continue();\n    });\n\n    await startRecording(page);\n    await simulateSpeech(page, 'do not send');\n    await stopRecording(page);\n    await page.click('#discard-btn');\n\n    // No message in history\n    await expect(page.locator('.message')).toHaveCount(0);\n    expect(requestMade).toBe(false);\n  });\n\n  test('sent message text is HTML-escaped in the DOM', async ({ mockPage: page }) => {\n    await page.route(WEBHOOK, (route) => route.fulfill({ status: 200, body: 'ok' }));\n    await page.evaluate((url) => localStorage.setItem('webhookUrl', url), WEBHOOK);\n    await page.reload();\n\n    await startRecording(page);\n    await simulateSpeech(page, '<b>bold</b>');\n    await stopRecording(page);\n    await page.click('#send-btn');\n\n    const msg = page.locator('.message').first();\n    // Text content should be the literal string, not rendered HTML\n    await expect(msg.locator('.message-text')).toHaveText('<b>bold</b>');\n    // No <b> element should be injected inside message text\n expect(page.locator('#messages-empty')).toBeVisible();\n\n    await startRecording(page);\n    await simulateSpeech(page, 'hide empty state');\n    await stopRecording(page);\n    await page.click('#send-btn');\n\n    await expect(page.locator('#messages-empty')).toBeHidden();\n  });\n});\n"

Write TOOL CALL 
{
  "file_path": "/home/gem/project/tests/pwa.spec.ts",
  "content": "/**\n * PWA Tests\n * Verifies manifest, service worker registration, and installability criteria.\n */\nimport { test as base, expect } from '@playwright/test';\nimport { APP_URL, MOCK_SPEECH_SCRIPT } from './fixtures';\n\nconst test = base;\n\ntest.describe('PWA manifest', () => {\n  test('manifest.json is accessible', async ({ page }) => {\n    const res = await page.request.get(`${APP_URL}/manifest.json`);\n    expect(res.status()).toBe(200);\n  });\n\n  test('manifest has correct name and short_name', async ({ page }) => {\n    const res = await page.request.get(`${APP_URL}/manifest.json`);\n    const manifest = await res.json();\n    expect(manifest.name).toBe('Hey Chill');\n    expect(manifest.short_name).toBe('Chill');\n  });\n\n  test('manifest has display: standalone', async ({ page }) => {\n    const res = await page.request.get(`${APP_URL}/manifest.json`);\n    const manifest = await res.json();\n    expect(manifest.display).toBe('standalone');\n  });\n\n  test('manifest has at least two icons', async ({ page }) => {\n    const res = await page.request.get(`${APP_URL}/manifest.json`);\n    const manifest = await res.json();\n    expect(Array.isArray(manifest.icons)).toBe(true);\n    expect(manifest.icons.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(2);\n  });\n\n  test('manifest has start_url set to \"/\"', async ({ page }) => {\n    const res = await page.request.get(`${APP_URL}/manifest.json`);\n    const manifest = await res.json();\n    expect(manifest.start_url).toBe('/');\n  });\n\n  test('manifest icons include 192x192 and 512x512 sizes', async ({ page }) => {\n    const res = await page.request.get(`${APP_URL}/manifest.json`);\n    const manifest = await res.json();\n    const sizes = manifest.icons.map((i: any) => i.sizes);\n    expect(sizes).toContain('192x192');\n    expect(sizes).toContain('512x512');\n  });\n});\n\ntest.describe('PWA service worker', () => {\n  test('service worker script is accessible', async ({ page }) => {\n    const res = await page.request.get(`${APP_URL}/sw.js`);\n    expect(res.status()).toBe(200);\n  });\n\n  test('service worker is registered after page load', async ({ page }) => {\n    await page.addInitScript(MOCK_SPEECH_SCRIPT);\n    await page.goto(APP_URL);\n\n    // Wait for service worker to register\n    const swState = await page.evaluate(async () => {\n      if (!('serviceWorker' in navigator)) return 'unsupported';\n      const reg = await navigator.serviceWorker.getRegistration('/');\n      return reg ? reg.scope : 'not-registered';\n    });\n\n    expect(swState).not.toBe('unsupported');\n    expect(swState).not.toBe('not-registered');\n  });\n});\n\ntest.describe('PWA meta tags', () => {\n  test('page links to manifest.json', async ({ page }) => {\n    await page.addInitScript(MOCK_SPEECH_SCRIPT);\n    await page.goto(APP_URL);\n    const manifestHref = await page.locator('link[rel=\"manifest\"]').getAttribute('href');\n    expect(manifestHref).toBeTruthy();\n  });\n});\n"
}

  "content": {
    "type": "tool_use",

    "name": "Write",
    "input": {
      "file_path": "/home/gem/project/tests/pwa.spec.ts",
      "content": "/**\n * PWA Tests\n * Verifies manifest, service worker registration, and installability criteria.\n */\nimport { test as base, expect } from '@playwright/test';\nimport { APP_URL, MOCK_SPEECH_SCRIPT } from './fixtures';\n\nconst test = base;\n\ntest.describe('PWA manifest', () => {\n  test('manifest.json is accessible', async ({ page }) => {\n    const res = await page.request.get(`${APP_URL}/manifest.json`);\n    expect(res.status()).toBe(200);\n  });\n\n  test('manifest has correct name and short_name', async ({ page }) => {\n    const res = await page.request.get(`${APP_URL}/manifest.json`);\n    const manifest = await res.json();\n    expect(manifest.name).toBe('Hey Chill');\n    expect(manifest.short_name).toBe('Chill');\n  });\n\n  test('manifest has display: standalone', async ({ page }) => {\n    const res = await page.request.get(`${APP_URL}/manifest.json`);\n    const manifest = await res.json();\n    expect(manifest.display).toBe('standalone');\n  });\n\n  test('manifest has at least two icons', async ({ page }) => {\n    const res = await page.request.get(`${APP_URL}/manifest.json`);\n    const manifest = await res.json();\n    expect(Array.isArray(manifest.icons)).toBe(true);\n    expect(manifest.icons.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(2);\n  });\n\n  test('manifest has start_url set to \"/\"', async ({ page }) => {\n    const res = await page.request.get(`${APP_URL}/manifest.json`);\n    const manifest = await res.json();\n    expect(manifest.start_url).toBe('/');\n  });\n\n  test('manifest icons include 192x192 and 512x512 sizes', async ({ page }) => {\n    const res = await page.request.get(`${APP_URL}/manifest.json`);\n    const manifest = await res.json();\n    const sizes = manifest.icons.map((i: any) => i.sizes);\n    expect(sizes).toContain('192x192');\n    expect(sizes).toContain('512x512');\n  });\n});\n\ntest.describe('PWA service worker', () => {\n  test('service worker script is accessible', async ({ page }) => {\n    const res = await page.request.get(`${APP_URL}/sw.js`);\n    expect(res.status()).toBe(200);\n  });\n\n  test('service worker is registered after page load', async ({ page }) => {\n    await page.addInitScript(MOCK_SPEECH_SCRIPT);\n    await page.goto(APP_URL);\n\n    // Wait for service worker to register\n    const swState = await page.evaluate(async () => {\n      if (!('serviceWorker' in navigator)) return 'unsupported';\n      const reg = await navigator.serviceWorker.getRegistration('/');\n      return reg ? reg.scope : 'not-registered';\n    });\n\n    expect(swState).not.toBe('unsupported');\n    expect(swState).not.toBe('not-registered');\n  });\n});\n\ntest.describe('PWA meta tags', () => {\n  test('page links to manifest.json', async ({ page }) => {\n    await page.addInitScript(MOCK_SPEECH_SCRIPT);\n    await page.goto(APP_URL);\n    const manifestHref = await page.locator('link[rel=\"manifest\"]').getAttribute('href');\n    expect(manifestHref).toBeTruthy();\n  });\n});\n"
    },
    "caller": {
      "type": "direct"
    }
  }
}

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #87
Now let me count the tests I have: 1. ui.spec.ts - 8 tests 2. settings.spec.ts - 8 tests 3. push-to-talk.spec.ts - 8 tests 4. transcription.spec.ts - 8 tests 5. vad.spec.ts - 5 tests 6. webhook.spec.ts - 9 tests (I added an extra one about "empty messages area") 7. pwa.spec.ts - let me count: - PWA manifest: 6 tests - PWA service worker: 2 tests - PWA meta tags: 1 test Total: 9 tests That gives me 55 tests total, which is one more than I originally planned for, but that's acceptable. Now I'm running the tests to see what passes, but I'm noticing a potential issue with how the settings modal is initialized. The modal overlay doesn't have a `hidden` attribute in the HTML itself, so I need to check what CSS or JavaScript is controlling its visibility state when the app loads. I need to trace through the modal's initial state more carefully. The HTML doesn't have a `hidden` attribute initially, so it's likely hidden by CSS by default. When settings is clicked, `openSettings()` removes the hidden attribute, making it visible. Then when closing, `closeSettings()` sets it back to hidden. My test assertions need to account for this state transition properly. The `hidden` attribute works differently than CSS—setting `hidden = true` actually adds the attribute to the element, which prevents rendering entirely, while the initial state relies on CSS `display: none` to hide it. So the flow is: initially hidden by CSS with no attribute, then `hidden = false` removes the attribute to show it, and `hidden = true` adds the attribute back to hide it again. The settings modal appears in the accessibility tree only after clicking, which suggests it's hidden by CSS initially rather than having a `hidden` attribute. When `closeSettings()` runs and sets `modalOverlay.hidden = true`, it adds the attribute, but the CSS might not be properly configured to hide the element based on that attribute, which could explain why it remains visible. I'm writing a test that checks the `hidden` attribute state before and after closing the modal... I'm realizing the CSS strategy might be backwards—if the modal starts visible by default in CSS and relies on the `hidden` attribute to hide it, then setting `hidden = false` wouldn't actually show it since there's no attribute to remove. The modal would need to start with `hidden` already set, or the CSS needs to check for the absence of the attribute to determine visibility. Looking back at the accessibility snapshot, the modal-overlay wasn't even listed, which suggests it's hidden with `display: none` by CSS initially. So when the settings button is clicked and `hidden = false` is called, that's not actually doing anything if there's no `hidden` attribute to remove in the first place—the CSS rule would still be hiding it. I'm realizing the CSS might use a default `display: none` with an `.open` class that switches it to `display: flex`, but the JavaScript I'm looking at is toggling the `hidden` attribute instead—so something doesn't add up. Let me just pull the CSS file directly to see what's actually controlling the visibility.
